inscription.head:
No. 219.
(B.K. No. 116 of 1926-27.)
HOSŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Trikūṭēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1129, Prabhava, Āshāḍha śu. 11, Sunday, Karkāṭaka-saṅkramaṇa.
There was an Adhika-Āshāḍha month in that year; but as there is no specific
mention of it, the month may be taken to be Nija-Āshāḍha. The said tithi thus
corresponds to 1207 A.D., July 7, the weekday being Saturday. The Karkāṭaka-saṅ-
kramaṇa did not occur on this day.

It records a gift of land to the God Mōrakēśvaradēva by Mahāpradhāna, Sarvā-
dhikāri Chikka-Narasiṅgayya, the second son of the king, who was governing Muḷugunda
Twelve. The gift land was secured by the donor from the Seven Hundred Mahājanas
of Hosavūru, headed by Mahābaḷa-gāvuṇḍa.
The Inscription is published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XXXII, pp. 171.

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Pēṭe Basappa temple.

This is dated Śaka 1128, Prabhava, Pushya śu 8, Monday, Uttarāyana-saṅkra-
maṇa. The date is irregular. The said tithi corresponds to 1207 A.D., December 2,
which was Friday and not Monday. The Saṅkramaṇa occurred two days earlier, on
December 26.

It records the gift of land and money to the god Telligēśvara by Arjunaseṭṭi,
the Mummuridaṇḍas and others, after washing the feet of the Āchārya of the
temple, Bhāskarāchārya, disciple of Kēdāradēva, when Chāvuṇḍayya is said to have
been governing Muḷugunda Twelve in Beḷuvala-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1926-27.)
NĀGĀVI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Kōḍi Basavaṇṇa temple.

This is dated the 21st regnal year of the king, Prajāpati, Kārttika-puṇṇami,
Monday, corresponding to 1211 A.D., October 23. The weekday, however, was
Sunday. The reference to Saṅkramaṇa in the date appears to be conventional.

It records a gift of land to the God Mahābalēśvara of Nāgāvi for worship and
offerings by Dharmarāśi-paṇḍita, after washing the feet of Sōmēśvaradēva the
Āchārya of the temple. The land was purchased by the donor for this purpose.

l.note: The letter pa is written upside down.
The letter va is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into a platform of Viśvanātha temple.

This is dated the 22nd regnal year of the king, Āṅgirasa, Vaiśākha śu. 13, Sunday,
corresponding to 1212 A.D., April 15.

It records a gift of gold to the god Madhusūdana by a Seṭṭi in the presence of the
One-Thousand Mahājanas of Lokkiguṇḍi. Other details are not known, as the record
is damaged.

edition.h4: TEXT. This is engraved in continuation of No. 61 above.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(B.K. No. 142 of 1926-27.)
BAḶAGĀNŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the chāvaḍi.

The partially preserved details of the date in this damaged record are Śaka 1 • • •,
Mārgaśira-puṇṇami.

It records a grant of land and house-site by Beḷḷaḷa Nāga-gāvuṇḍa, for a fee-
ding house (satra) at Baḷaganūru. The gift was entrusted to the ascetic Chandra-
bhūshaṇa-yati, whose virtues are extolled at length.

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(B.K. No. 195 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the prākāra of Amṛitēśvara temple.

The inscription belongs to the reign of Vīra-Ballāḷa II. It describes the subordi-
nate chief Maṇḍalika Bāchaṇa who was governing the tract from the rājadhāni-paṭṭaṇa
Aṇṇigeṟe. The purpose of the record seems to be to register a gift to the god Amṛitēś-
vara. The epigraph being badly damaged and worn out, most of the details are lost.

l.ka:
jananutanappa maṇḍaḷika bācaṇavallabhagau • • • • • • • • • • ruṁnati
yoḷa pa-

l.note: The rest of the record is broken off.

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 470 of 1926.)
AḶNĀVAR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying on a raised pial of the Jumma-masjid in Pyāṭi Street.

This is dated Śaka 1003, Wednesday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkramaṇa, other details
being lost. In the Śaka year 1003 corresponding to 1081 A.D., the Dakshiṇāyana-
saṅkramaṇa occurred on June 26, Saturday.

It is known that Chālukya Vikramāditya VI was the imperial ruler at this time.
But the inscription without mentioning his name states that the feudatory
chief Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gūvaladēva of the Goa Kadamba family was governing the
provinces of Koṅkaṇa Nine-hundred, Palasige Twelve-thousand, Kundūr Five-hund-
red, Kādaravaḷḷi Thirty, Kavadi-dvīpa One lakh and a quarter and other areas. The
Kadamba Praśasti is recounted.

It registers a gift of land made after purchase by Narasiṅga-seṭṭi in favour of the
Biḷiya-basadi which was constructed at the rājadhāni Aṇalāpura by Pradhāna Mācha-
ṇṇa. It mentions the Jaina preceptors Rāmachandra and Bāḷachandra. The epigraph
is worn out in certain places.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1926.)
AḶNĀVAR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone lying in the compound of the Baraguṇḍi house.

It refers to the king Gōvala, apparently of the Goa Kadamba family, and describes
the loyalty and valour of a warrior who seems to have died in a fight. The inscription
is much damaged, the details being lost.

l.note: The continuation is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(B.K. No. 454 of 1926.)
VENKAṬĀPUR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the temple.

The details of date preserved in this damaged and worn-out record are Śaka
105[8] and Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti corresponding to 1136 A.D. December 25.

In the earlier portion of the epigraph, which appears to have contained a reference
to the rule of a Chālukya suzerain, mention is made of a gift by Padmaladēvī. Subse-
quently it seems to record a gift made either by the Kadamba chief Jayakēśin II and
his queen Maiḷala-dēvī or by some one at their instance. There is reference to rājadhāni
Bhōgū[ru].

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 429 of 1926.)
AMMINBHĀVI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Mallikārjuna temple.

This is dated the 17th regnal year of the king, Śukla, Pushya-puṇṇame, Sunday,
lunar eclipse. The date is irregular. In the year Śukla falling in the reign period of the
king, the said tithi would correspond to 1149 A.D., December 16 which was Friday.
Lunar eclipse did not occur on this day. This date would show that his first regnal
year was counted from 1133 A.D., falling within the reign period of his father. The
king is stated as ruling over the provinces of Gōve, Koṅkaṇa, Sēvurige-nāḍu and Hala-
sige-nāḍu.

It registers a gift of land and two houses made in favour of the god Mallikārjuna
and two maṭhas by Māḍa Boppaṇa. The donor is said to have purchased the gift
property from the lords of Beḷavāḍigēri which formed part of Ammanabāvi, an agrahāra
under the administration of Four Hundred Mahājanas.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 464 of 1926.)
SIDDHĀPUR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Siddēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1080, Bahudhānya, Āshāḍha-amāvāsyā, Monday, Dakshi,
ṇāyana-saṅkramaṇa, corresponding to 1158 A.D., June 27. The weekday, however,
was Friday.

The inscription recounts the Kadamba praśasti and states that Mahāmaṇḍa-
lēśvara Śivachitta-Vīra-Permāḍi and Yuvarāja-Kumāra Vijayāditya were governing
Palasige Twelve-Thousand and Koṅkaṇa Nine-Hundred from Saṁpagāḍi. It registers
gifts for worship, offerings and repairs in the temple of the god Pañchaliṅga of Hosavoḷal
situated in Kittūr Thirty by the four Prabhus, Aṟuvattokkalu and Five Hundred
Tōṇṭigas.

l.ka: kittūru mūvattara baḷiya bāḍa hosavaḷala śrī

l.note: The remaining pertion is broken off.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1926.)
BOKYAPUR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Vīrabhadra temple.

This is dated the 17th regnal year of the king, Subhānu, Pushya śu. 10, Monday,
Saṅkramaṇa. The date is irregular. In the said cyclic year corresponding to 1163
A.D., the tithi corresponded to December 7 which was Saturday. The Saṅkramaṇa,
obviously Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, occurred on December 26, Thursday.

It records a gift of wet land for worship, offerings and burning a lamp, to the god
Svayaṁbhu of Arasigeṟe, by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Śivachitta-Vīra-Permāḍi who was
governing Koṁkaṇa Nine-Hundred and Halasige Twelve-Thousand from Gōve. The
gift was entrusted into the hands of Śivaśakti-paṇḍita, disciple of Kēdāraśakti-paṇḍita.
Two more gifts to the deity are also mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1926.)
AMMINBHĀVI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in the Kalamēśvara temple.

This is dated the 18th regnal year of the chief, Śōbhakṛit, Chaitra śu. 11, Tuesday.
The cyclic year corresponds to 1183-84 A.D. But the details of the date are irregular.
Chaitra śu. 11 of that year corresponded to March 6, the weekday being Sunday.
Śivachitta Permāḍi of the Kadamba family of Goa who also bore the title Tribhu-
vanamalla, is known to have commenced his reign from 1147 A.D. Thus the regnal
year cited would be too low for his reign.

It describes an officer and records his death in a fight. The epigraph is worn
out and no other details can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 434 of 1926.)
DĒVARA HUBBAḶḶI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in front of the Kōḍi Basavaṇṇa temple.

This is dated the 20th regnal year of the king, Kshaya, Jyēshṭha śu. 8, Wednesday
corresponding to 1206 A.D., May 17.

It states that the Mahājanas of Huppavaḷḷi had the temple of Mahālakshmī built
with the unanimous agreement and endowed land and a shop for worship and offerings
to the deity. It also records gifts including oil for burning a lamp before the deity,
by the Seṭṭiguttas and other members of the trading community of the four towns of
Dāravāḍa, Narēndrapura, Taḍakōḍu and Ammeyabāvi in Halasige Twelve-Thousand.
Other gifts by the One-Thousand, the Five-Hundred-and-four and the Seṭṭiguttas of
the four towns mentioned above, as well as others are also mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 437 of 1926.)
MANGUṆḌI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying near the ruined temple of Siddhaliṅgēśvara.

The record contains six dates. The first is the 16th year of the reign of Vīra-
Jayakēśidēva, commencing from the Kaliyuga year 4289, Dundubhi, Pushya
ba. 8, Wednesday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa. The Kaliyuga year cited is the year from
which the regnal year was reckoned. For the cyclic year Dundubhi falling within the
reign of the Kadamba king, the details correspond to 1203 A.D., January 7. The
weekday, however, was Tuesday.

The second date which is the cyclic year Rudhirōdgāri, Śrāvaṇa-pūrṇami,
Friday, corresponds to 1203 A.D., July 25. The third date is the 25th
year of the reign of Vīra-Jayakēśidēva, commencing from Kaliyuga year 4288, Prajāpati,
Śrāvaṇa ba. 13, Monday. This corresponds to 1211 A.D., August 8, the year being
current. The fourth date is the 28th year of the reign of Vīra-Jayakēśidēva, coM-
mencing from the Kaliyuga year 4289, Bhāva, Māgha ba. 1, Saturday, which
corresponds to 1215 A.D., January 17, Saturday, f.d.t. °21. The fifth date is the
2nd regnal year of Vīra-Vajradēva, Dhātu, Śrāvaṇa śu. 5, Thursday, corresponding
to 1216 A.D., July 2. The last date is the 2nd regnal year of Vīra-Vajradēva. Dhātu,
Mārgasīrsha śu [1] the weekday being lost. The details cannot be verified but it
falls in the month of November in 1216 A.D.

The inscription commences with the formal Chālukya praśasti and refers to the
reign of Tribhuvanamalla or Sōmēśvara IV who was actually no more. After giving
a brief account of the Kadamba rulers of Goa, it introduces Jayakēśin III and his son
Vajradēva who were ruling over Palasige Nāḍu. Next is described a line of ascetics
who flourished at Maṇigundage in Hulambi Seventy. The last of them was Chandra-
bhūshaṇa. At the instance of this teacher a temple of Śiva was constructed with the
co-operation of the residents of the place and named Tribuvanavīra-Jayakēśi, also
called Siddhēśvara. Various gifts consisting of land, fixed shares of income, oil, etc.,
were made to this deity by Chauḍa-gāvuṇḍa, the Five-Hundred-and-four representa-
tives of the place and others and these were entrusted into the hands of the above
named teacher who was the trustee of the temple. Among such donors are mentioned
the Three Hundred of Muguda, Ugura Three Hundred of Huppavaḷḷi, Nuggiyahaḷḷi,
Sattivūr and the others of Kandagallu. Nandigāve, Sonnaligeyapura, Vijayāpura,
Ayyāvaḷe and Khēḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1926.)
AḶNĀVAR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab in front of the Kalamēśvara temple.

The epigraph contains a part of the Kadamba praśasti. From an invocation to
the ina-śāsana in the beginning, it appears to have recorded a gift to a Jaina temple.
The inscription is badly damaged and fragmentary.

l.note: The rest of

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(B.K. No. 164 of 1926-27.)
KAṆAGINAHĀḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab built into the wall of the Nārāyaṇadēva temple.

The epigraph which is fragmentary contains the description of the Sinda family
commencing with Āchugi. It stops with the prose praśasti of Permāḍi, son of Āchugi
by Mahādēvi. The text is almost identical with that found in lines 20-37 of No. 119 of
South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XI, Part II.

l.note: The beginning is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(B.K. No. 147 of 1928-29.)
HOSŪR, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Siddhēśvara temple, two miles from the village.

This is dated Śaka 1076, Yuva, Pushya śu. 14, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti.
If śu. is a mistake for ba. and Monday for Sunday, then the details correspond to
1155 A.D., December 25. The Śaka year was current.

It records a gift of land to the god Mallikārjuna in Kapilāśrama by Gayādhara-
nāyaka, Pergaḍe Sōmanātha-nāyaka, Karaṇa Rāyarasa and others with the approval
of the Sinda queen Lakshmidēvī, wife of Chāvuṇḍa. Lakshmīdēvī is said to have been
administering the area granted by her husband.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(B.K. No. 28 of 1927-28.)
UṆACHGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up near the public well.

This is dated the 10th regnal year of the king, Pārthiva, Pushya śu. 13, Sunday
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa. The date is not regular. The year Pārthiva falling in
the reign of Sinda Chāvuṇḍa corresponds to 1165 A.D. In that year Pushya śu.
occurred on December 17, the weekday being Friday. The said Saṁkramaṇa how-
over, occurred on December 25, Saturday.

It records a gift by Heggade Īśvarayya, of the Hejjuṅka and Bīravaṇa taxes,
under the instructions of Chāvuṇḍarasa. The latter part of the record is damaged.

edition.h4: TEXT. Engraved in continuation of No. 122 of South Indian Inscriptions. Vol. XI, Part II.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(B.K. No. 4 of 1927-28.)
HIRĒMAṆṆŪR, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the wall of Hanumān temple.

This is dated Śaka 1091, Virōdhi, Pushya śu. 5, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkra-
maṇa, corresponding to 1169 A.D., December 25.

It records the renewal of a gift of land for the maintenance of a tank called Hiriya-
keṟe of Hiriya-Maṇiyūr, at the request of Dāsabōva, the Adhishṭhāyaka of Jinaśāle, by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chāvuṇḍa of the Sinda family who was governing Kisukāḍu
Seventy, Bāgaḍage Seventy; Nareyaṁgal Twelve and Keḷavāḍi Three Hundred.
The earlier part gives a genealogical account of the donor and his exploits.

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(B.K. No. 107 of 1926-27.)
HOSŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the wall of the Śiva temple.

It records the gift of income derived from the taxes Hejjuṅka and Bīravaṇa for
lamp, worship and offerings, to the god Mūlasthana, by Nāgadēva and Kēśava, Peg-
gaes of the tax officer Bīcharasa. The latter is stated to have been a subordinate
of the governor of the area, Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chāvuṇḍa who might be the Sinda chief
of that name.

l.ka: daḷīdaṁḍavēdaṁḍaṁ kadanapracaṁḍaṁ nuḍidantegaṇḍaṁ vīra cāvuṇḍarasaru || ka || sthiradiṁ
suṁkada bīcarasaṁ vibhudaikamaṁḍanaṁ dharegesadaṁ || ·ātana pe¨rggaḍegaḷu vikhyātaya-

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1926-27.)
HARTI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the temple of Īśvara.

This is dated the 7th regnal year of the Sinda feudatory, Vijaya, Kārttika-puṇṇami,
Monday, corresponding to 1173 A.D., October 22.

It records the gift of income from the Bīravaṇa tax on the garden of the god to the
god Gōyidēśvara by the twelve gāvuṇḍas of Harati, Aṟuvattokkalu, Ugura Three-
Hundred, the One Thousand and Heggaḍe Remmarasa after washing the feet of Jāta-
vēda-paṇḍita, the Āchārya of the temple. Another gift to the same god by Maduvarasa
is also registered.

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(B.K. No. 205 of 1926-27.)
NIḌGUNDI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone inside the Basavaṇṇa temple.

This is dated Śaka 1096, Jaya, Mārggaśīra-paurṇami, Monday, lunar eclipse,
corresponding to 1174 A.D., November 10. The weekday, however, was Sunday.
The Sinda chief Vīra-Bijjaḷa stated to have been ruling at this time.

It records a gift of money made into the hands of the Mahājanas in favour of the
god Mādhavēśvara whose temple was constructed at Niḍugundi by the donor Karaga-
vaḷaradhishṭhāyaka Mahādēva-daṇḍanāyaka, who is described as the right hand of
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Bijjala. It also registers other gifts including one for the
maintenance of a satra. The earlier part of the inscription contains a genealogical
account of the Sinda chief and a description of the Four Hundred Mahājanas of
Niḍugundi. The epigraph is partly damaged.

l.note: The remaining portion is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(B.K. No. 65 of 1928-29.)
SŪḶIBHĀVI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone pillar in the Tuḷajābhavāni temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Viśvāvasu, Māgha-amāvāsye, Monday. The said year,
falling in the regime of Vīra-Bijjaḷa and Vīra-Vikrama of the Sinda family, corresponds
to 1185-86 A.D. The equivalent of the date would be 1186 A.D., February 21, the
weekday being Friday. If, however, Viśvāvasu is a mistake for Parābhava, the details
correspond to 1187 A.D., February 9, Monday, f.d.t. °31. This seems to be the intended
date.

It records a gift of land for maintaining the sluice of a tank by Mahāmaṇḍalēś-
vara Sinda Vīra-Bijjaḷa and Vīra-Vikramāditya. In the latter part of the record
which is damaged is registered another gift by two toll officers of Kisukāḍu Nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(B.K. No. 222 of 1926-27.)
ABBĪGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken stone lying below a tamarind tree at Chaukimaṭha.

This damaged record contains the following details of the date: Mārgaśira śu.
7, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa. It cannot be verified.

It records two land gifts. The second is a gift of land to the god Mūlasthāna after
washing the feet of the preceptor Mallikārjuna by the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvaras Vīra-Bijjaṇa
and Vikramāditya of the Sinda family who were rulingfrom their capital at Eramba-
rage. The earlier portion which is damaged contains references to Chāmuṇḍarasa,
Kisukāḍu, Abbayigeṟe and a gift the details being lost.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(B.K. No. 223 of 1926-27.)
ABBĪGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the front wall of the house of Gurāchārya Pañchamukhi.

This is dated Śaka 1301, Siddhārthi, Jyēsṭha śu. 15, Tuesday, lunar eclipse,
corresponding to 1379 A.D., May 31.

It records a gift of land in Avvegēri to Hēmādriyāchārya of Yarigēri by the king.
The record is in Nāgari characters, the language being Kannaḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(B.K. No. 37-A of 1927-28.)
KAḶAKAPPANA-GUḌḌA, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a stone lying against the wall at the left of the entrance into the central shrine
of Kālakālēśvara temple.

This worn out epigraph is engraved below No. 165 above. It mentions Bomma-
[gauḍa].

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(B.K. No. 3 of 1927-28.)
SAVAḌI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Brahmadēva temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Virōdhi, Pushya śu. 15, the Śaka year being lost.
The name of the king, who might be Dēvarāya II, is not fully preserved and the cyclic
year may be a mistake for Virōdhikṛit. In that case the date of the inscription
would be 1431 A.D., December 19.

It records the renovation of the temple of Traimūrti at Saiviḍi in Bādāvi-sīme
by Chavuḍaya-nāyaka, son of Tipaṇa-nāyaka of Bāgilavāḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(B.K. No. 117 of 1929-30.)
BAILŪR, HONAWAR TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab standing near the Haslara huts.

This is dated Śaka 1356, Pramādi, Kārttika ba. 10, the weekday being lost. This
corresponds to 1433 A.D., November 6. The Śaka year was current.

It records an endowment made in favour of Nārāyaṇabhaṭṭa of Gujjāḍi, son of
Kēśava, by Adhikāri Saṇṇu Nāyaru in conjunction with the pontiff Surēśvara-śrīpāda
and the people of the Bailūr region for the exposition of the Purāṇas every day in the
temple of Maḍakēśvara and also in the maṭha of the said teacher for thirty days in the
month of Sōṇe(Śrāvaṇa).

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(B.K. No. 118 of 1929-30.)
BAILŪR, HONAWAR TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab lying in the Mārkaṇḍēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1356, Pramādi, Kārttika ba. 10, Sunday. The datails corres-
ponds to 1433 A.D., November 6, the Śaka year being current. The weekday, however,
was Friday.

The contents of the record are the same as in No. 247 above. This seems to be
almost a copy of it.

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(B.K. No. 119 of 1929-30.)
CHTTRIGE, HONAWAR TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab standing in a field.

This gives the genealogical account of the Vijayanagara kings, in the course of
which are mentioned Bukkarāya, Harihara, Vijaya-bhūpati and Dēvarāya. It seems
to record a gift to the god Rāmachandra. It also mentions the village Chittārige,
situated in Honnāvura-dēśa, and the āchārya Rāmachandra-yatīśvara. The inscription
is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(B.K. No. 223 of 1928-29.)
GUṀGŌḶA, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 414 on the way to Ugaragōḷa.

This is dated Śaka 1451, Sarvadhāri, Māgha śu. 7, Saturday, corresponding to
1529 A.D., January 16. The Śaka year was current.

It refers to Vīra-Narasiṅganāyaka and the Durgādhipati of Toṟagale. Toṟagale-
vēṇThe and Guṁmagoḷa are mentioned. The inscription is much damaged and the
details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(B.K. No. 21 of 1927-28.)
GĀḌGŌḶI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab standing close to the Hanumān temple.

The record registers a gift for offerings and lamp to the god Hanumān. The inscrip-
tion is worn out and all other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(B.K. No. 7 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the prākāra wall of Vīranārāyaṇa temple.

This is dated Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Bhādrapada śu. 12, Tuesday, corresponding to
1569 A.D., August 26.

It records the gift of Ānandanidhi to the Brāhmaṇas, by the king in the presence of
the god Viṭhalēśvara on the bank of the Tuṅgabhadrā. It adds that the gift was
recorded on stone in the presence of the god Triyambaka of Gadagu.

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab built into the wall of an enclosure to the left of the well in the Vīra-
nārāyaṇa temple.

This is dated Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Bhādrapada śu. 12, Tuesday, corresponding to
1539 A.D., August 26.

It records the gift of Ānandanidhi to the Brāhmaṇas by the king, in the presence
of the god Viṭhalēśvara on the bank of the Tuṅgabhadrā. It adds that the gift was
recorded on stone in the presence of the god Vīranārāyaṇa who had conferred his boon
on the poet Kumāra-Vyāsa.

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(B.K. No. 186 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆĪGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab set up on the right side of the southern entrance into the
Amṛitēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Bhādrapada śu. 12, Tuesday, corresponding to
1539 A.D., August 26.

It records the gift of Ānandanidhi to the Brāhmaṇas by the king in the presence of
the god Viṭhalēśvara on the banks of the Tuṅgabhadrā. It adds that the gift was
recorded on stone in the presence of the god Amṛitēśvara of Aṇṇigeṟe. (Compare Nos.
252 and 253 above).

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(B.K. No. 99 of 1928-29.)
ANWĀL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into a water reservoir in Hanumantadēva temple.

The record mentions Anavāli, evidently modern Anwāl. The epigraph is badly
damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(B.K. No. 222 of 1928-29.)
GUḌISĀGARA, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the house of Haḍapada Mallēśappa.

This is dated in the cyclic year Plavaṅga, Āshāḍha śu. 10. The year fell in Śaka
1469 in the reign of Sadāśivarāya. The date corresponds to 1547 A.D., June 27.

It seems to record a gift to a barber. The details cannot be made out, as the
epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(B.K. No. 433 of 1926.)
AMMINBHĀVI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in the backyard of a barber's house.

This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Āshāḍha śu. 11, corresponding to 1547 A.D.,
June 28.

It records the remission of taxes on barbers of the Amminabhāvi area in Haṟagale-
sīme, at the representation of Timmōja, Koṇḍōja and Bhadrōja by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Śrīrangarāja and Rāmarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(B.K. No. 435 of 1926.)
DĒVARA HUBBAḶḶI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up at the entrance into the village.

This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Āśvija śu. 2, Thursday, corresponding to 1547
A.D., September 15, Thursday, f.d.t. °10.

It records the remission of taxes in favour of barbers represented by Koṇḍōja
Timmōja and Bhadrōja by Rāmarājayya, the Kāryakartta of the king. Reference
is made to Muguda Thirty and Kāṇāchī in the supplementary section.

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(B.K. No. 146 of 1928-29.)
HEBBAḶḶI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone in the Hanumantadēva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1470, Plavaṅga, Kārttika śu. 7, Friday, corresponding to 1547
A.D., October 21, Friday, f.d.t. °10. The Śaka year was current.

It records the remission of taxes on barbers by Rāmarāja and Timmarāja, on the
representation made by Timmōja, Koṇḍōja and Bhadrōja. The details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(B.K. No. 214 of 1928-29.)
BEḶVAṬIGI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of Rāmaliṅga temple.

This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, the tithi being 10. The details are lost. The
date corresponds approximately to 1547-48 A.D.

It records the remission of taxes in favour of the barbers of Toṟagala-sīme by
Rāmarāya and Veṅkaṭādri. Koṇḍōja and Sidōja of the community also received
some concessions.

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(B.K. No. 169 of 1926-27.)
KARḌI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Hanumān temple.

This is dated Śaka 1475, Pramādīcha, Āśvija ba. 3, Saturday, corresponding to
1553 A.D., September 23, Saturday f.d.t. °87.

It records the remission of taxes on the barbers of Mudugal-sīme at the request
of Timmōja, Koṇḍōja and Bhadrōja.

l.ka: hana śakavara śa 1475 neya pramādaca saṁ-
sīmege saluva karadāya samatige • yava nā-

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying near the boys' school.

It seems to record a gift to Timōja of Soraṭūr by the king's officer Rāmarāja.
The record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1927-28.)
YĀONGAL, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in Survey No. 133.

This is dated Śaka 1477, Āṅgirasa, Kārttika śu. 3. The date is irregular. Śaka
1477 corresponds to the cyclic year Rākshasa when the details agree to 1555 A.D.,
October 18.

It registers a gift of garden for maintaining a charitable picotta on the Haḍagali-
Ēvagal road by the administrative officer of Gadugu-sīme and the local officials under
instructions from Mahārājādhirāja Mallika Sāhēba.

l.note: This

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar called Mālagamba on the Siddhēśvara hill.

This is dated Śaka 1511, Virōdhi, Śrāvaṇa śu. 1, Thursday, corresponding to
1589 A.D., July 3.

It seems to record the setting up of two pillars for lamps by Khaṇḍērāva Timmāji
of Śainīka-gotra, a subordinate of Vajīr Haider Khan. The language of the record is
Marāṭhī and the script Nāgarī.

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into a wall of the well called Rāyarabhāvi.

This is dated Śaka 1526, Krōdhi, Māgha śu. 2, Friday, Dhanishṭhā-nakshatra,
The details of the date correspond to 1605 A.D., January 11. The Sultan of Bijāpur
is referred to as the son of Ali Ādilshāh.

It records the completion of a charitable well at Bīḷagi by Khaṇḍērāya-paṇḍita.
who was holding the ṭhāṇe under Vajīra, Khāne-Ajam, Yākūta-khāna who was in
charge of the Bīḷagi-samata as Mokhāsi. It is stated that the digging of the well was
started on Śaka 1525, Śubhakrit, Adhika-Āshāḍha paurṇami, Monday, Mūlā-
nakshatra, corresponding to 1603 A.D., June 13. The cyclic year was, however,
Śōbhana. Cf. No. 264 below.

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(B.K. No. 71 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another stone in the same place.

This record in Marāṭhi language and Nāgarī characters, is another version of the
above inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(B.K. No. 96 of 1928-29.)
ĀLŪR, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 91.

It states that Salabhatakhān Sāheb conferred upon Dēvaṇagauḍa of Ālūru
the honour of riding a horse with a parasol and a gift of garden free of tax.

edition.h4: TEXT. At the bottom is engraved the figure of a horse and a person holding a

l.ka: ga tōṭa vināti koṭu satigeyavana jāvaka

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(B.K. No. 202 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near a well called Huḍeda-bhāvi.

This is dated Śaka 1567, Vyaya, Mārgaśira śu. 3, Sunday, corresponding to 1646
A.D., November 29, Sunday, f.d.t. °32. The Śaka year was current.

The ruling king is apparently Muhammad Shah, the Ādilshāhi Sultau who is
referred to by the Hindu praśasti and stated to be the overlord of the kingdom of
Vidyāpura, i.e., Bijapur.

It records the grant of a cowl exempting the taxes for a period of twelve years
to the chiefs and representatives of Rustumpēṭhe, newly built in Aṇṇigere-sīme by
Rustummajamasāhēba, son of Raṇadullākāna-sāhēba, who was the agent of the Sultan.

l.note: Readsultāna hamada bādaśāha.

l.ka: lla pasikiyilla yi kaulige tappi[da]vara bā-

l.note: This line is engraved in the left margin.

edition.p: 1. These two lines are engraved on the left side of the same slab.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(B.K. No. 50 of 1927-28.)
KALKĒRI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the village chāvaḍi.

This is dated Śaka 1573, Khara, Chaitra ba. 5, corresponding to 1651 A.D., March
31. The weekday is not mentioned.

It records a kowl granted by the Muslim officer of Kalikere-sīme exempting tax
on the construction of new houses for a period of seven years. The details are not
clear.

l.ka: ra sīme sāhēbaru rā || ra || ma || muṁ-
ṭṭi koṭṭaṇa māpu yēḷu varuṣa tu-
li haṁdi koṁda hāge brāhmarige kāsīli

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(B.K. No. 218 of 1928-29.)
BHADRĀPUR, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the village chāvaḍi.

This is dated Śaka 1601, Siddhārthi, Śrāvaṇa śu. 4, corresponding to 1679 A.D.,
July 31 (not verifiable).

It records the grant of the privileges of Gauḍike and Śānabōvike to Saṁgappa.
It mentions the temples of Haṇamanta and Saṅkaraliṅga. The record is damaged
and indifferently engraved.

l.note: The rest of

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(B.K. No. 47 and 48 of 1928-29.)
ILAKAL, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the fort wall.

This record in Nāgarī characters is dated Śaka 1725, Śrēshṭha (Jyēshṭha) ba. 3 (?)
Wednesday. These details in the Śaka year which coincided with Rudhirōdgāri,
correspond to 1803 A.D., June 8. The record eites some more details of date which
seem to have been engraved wrongly.

It records the construction of the gate-way at Hunagunda by the local residents
headed by the Gauḍas etc., with the approval of the administrative authorities including
the Collector and the Mahālakarī. The epigraph refers to the rule of the Kupaṇi
Sarakāra, i.e., East India Company.

l.de: ta pakṣe triśatpakhyā mite saumye di-

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(B.K. No. 239 of 1927-28.)
HAVĒLI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of Māruti temple.

This is dated Śaka 1744 and San 1232. The Muslim reckoning might be Sūr-san.
The date may be equated to 1822 A.D.

It states that Appurāya who was the Subhēdāra of Bāgaḍikōti under the Kumpaṇi
Sarakāra, i.e., East India Company, rehabilitated the village of Havēli lying waste
for many years, renamed it as Gōpālapura, constructed the temples of Hanumān and
Śiva and made provision for worship and offerings therein. The epigraph is written
in Nāgarī characters and Sanskrit language.

l.de: bdameva patito grāmo havelyākhyayuk tasyotdhāramatho vi-
dhāya bhuvi taṁ gopālapūḥsaṁjñakaṁ || kṛtvā tatra hanūmadīśanilayaṁ ni-
mayi vāpīṁ sudhī tatpārśvadvitaye vaṭāmrasurabhīn bodhidrumān kiṁśu-
kṛtau viprāṇāṁ pratihāyanapratidinaṁ vṛttiṁ prakalpyādhunā || śrīdeva-
sya mahotsavāya surathāṁstvaiśīṁ tathā vaiṭṭhalīṁ gādhitvā pratimāṁ cakā-
ra sarasīṁ tāṁ caṁdrabhāgāsamāṁ || [4] || madvaṁśajā paramahīpati

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1928-29.)
KELŪR, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone in front of the village office.

This is dated Fasli 1234, Tāraṇa, Āsvija śu. 5. The said Fasli might be Sūr-san.
The date may be equated to 1824 A.D., September 27.

It refers to the conquest of Kellūr by the Kumpaṇi-sarakāra, i.e., East India
Company.

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(B.K. No. 58 of 1928-29.)
KELŪR, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another stone in front of the village office.
This is copy of the above record.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(B.K. No. 240 of 1927-28.)
HONNAKAṬṬI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On three pieces of a broken slab kept in the Māruti temple.

This is dated Śaka 1750, Sarvadhāri, Āśvija, Vijaya-daśamī. The details corres-
pond to 1828 A.D., October 13.

It states that the village of Honnāyakanakaṭi located to the east of Bāgaḍi-
kōṭi, had been deserted for a long time and that it was rehabilitated by Gopālappa,
grandson of Rāyappa of Vaḍavāḍige, who also constructed a temple and had the
old tank repaired. The officers of the East India Company who were pleased
with this work had it recorded on stone. The record is in Nāgari characters and
Kannaḍa language.

l.de: mma hireru māḍida jīrṇa taṭāka bhāvi devālaya ka-
ṭisi toṭamāḍi • • • ya karma noḍi parituṣṭarāda kuṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(B.K. No. 157 of 1928-29.)
MAHĀKŪṬA, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in a porch of the Mahākūṭēśvara temple.

It describes the benevolent administration, valour and generosity of a Mahāsā-
manta.
Compare Indian Antiquary, Vol. X, pp. 104 ff.

group.h3:
No. 277-400.
(B.K. Nos. 93 to 216 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
The following series of short inscriptions mostly contain individual names or
epithets, some of which are interesting.
On the rock above the entrance into cave No. 1.

inscription.head: No. 281.
inscription.p: On a mutilated pillar in the verandah of the same cave.

edition.p: On the side to the proper right of the same cave.
On the side of a cavern to the proper right of the same cave.
On the celling of the same cavern.
(Note: These five inscriptions are in Nāgarī script).

inscription.head: No. 295.
inscription.p: On a rock to the right of the steps leading up to cave No. II.

edition.p: On another rock to the left of the same steps.
On the hill-side to the proper left of the same cave.
On the hill-side above the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 317.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the verandah of the same cave.

edition.p: On the rock to the proper right of the hill-side above the same cave.
On the hill-side to the proper right of the same place.

inscription.head: No. 326.
inscription.p: On the big rock in front of cave No II.

edition.p: On the small rock in the same place.

(Note: The above is engraved in medieval characters).
On the hill-side to the right of the steps leading up to cave No. III.
On a rock to the right of the same steps.
(Note: This is engraved inversely in the negative form).

inscription.head: No. 350.
inscription.p:
On the hill-side to the right of the foot-path leading up the hill from the right
of the same cave.

edition.p: On the rock to the proper right of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 353.
inscription.p: On the hill-side in the same place.

inscription.head: No. 354.
inscription.p: On the rock in the same place.

inscription.head: No. 355.
inscription.p: On the rock to the proper right of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 356.
inscription.p: On the hill-rock in the same place.

edition.p: On the hill-side in the same place.
On the rock in the same place.
On the hill-side in the same place.
On the hill-side to the proper left of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 368.
inscription.p: On the top of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 369.
inscription.p: On the rock above the same cave.

edition.p: On the top of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 372.
inscription.p: On the rock to the proper right of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 373.
inscription.p: On the rock to the right of the way leading up to cave No. IV.

inscription.head: No. 374.
inscription.p: Below a sculpture on a rock to the proper left of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 375.
inscription.p: On the higher hill-rock to the proper left of the same cave.

edition.p: On the hill-side to the proper left of the same cave.

In the cavern to the right of the foot-path leading up the hill, from the
right of the same cave.

inscription.head: No. 400.
inscription.p: On the basement of the same cave.

group.h3:
No. 401-411.
(B.K. No. 219 of 229 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
The first three of these short inscriptions mention Eraṇḍara Gaṇacha who is des-
ribed as the author of certain sculptures. The fifth states that Chandrakīrti-bhaṭṭa
arved the figure of the goddess Durgā. The next one is partially preserved. The rest
eem to constitute individual names or epithets.
In the cavern of the hill-rock to the proper left of cave No. IV.

inscription.head: No. 407.
inscription.p: Between the two rows of sculptures in the same cavern.

inscription.head: No. 408.
inscription.p: In the same cavern.

edition.p: On a rock near the tank.

group.h3:
No. 412-414.
(B.K. Nos. 231 to 233 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
The first of these three inscriptions seems to describe the valour of Paradara
Gōṇi. The second contains a funny reference to Vakkasidēva. The third mentions
the name Bariṇapāla.

inscription.head: No. 412.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Kallanātha-maṭha.

inscription.head: No. 413.
inscription.p: On another pillar.

inscription.head: No. 414.
inscription.p: On a third pillar.

group.h3:
No. 415-416.
(B.K. Nos. 235 and 236 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p: On the hill-side to the left of the way leading up to the Taṭṭu-Kōṭi Māruti temple.

These two label-inscriptions seem to contain the names Nandevajā and Puṁ-
balagi.

group.h3:
No. 417-447.
(B.K. No. 101 to 131 of 1928-29.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
The following series of short inscriptions are made up of names of individuals or
places, descriptive labels and references to works. Some of the names are interesting
and a few seem to bear historical associations; for instance, Śrī-Vikkarasan, Śrī-
Bhīmaśakti, Śrī Bhīmaśakti Śēndrakan and Śrī-Satyāśraya-pāda-yugakke bhaktan
Bhīmaśatti: the last of these refers to the Sēndraka chief Bhīmaśakti and describes
him as a devotee of the feet of the illustrious Satyāśraya.
On a rocky wall to the right of the passage leading up the northern fort.

edition.p: On the wall to the left of the same passage.

inscription.head: No. 429.
inscription.p: Teems to read: śrīripuraḍidēyōdiva[rē] kaścisādō

edition.p: On a cliff to the left of the bypath to the right of the same passage.

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(B.K. No. 449 of 1926.)
KALLE, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

It records the death of Baysara Sabbaya in an encounter following an assault on
the village of Doḍḍavāḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the back of a slab set up in front of the Vīrabhadra temple.
It mentions Dērakeṟegaya and seems to refer to a gift. The epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(B.K. No. 230 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Kallanātha maṭha.

It records the remission of a tax called Kavaḷte on the cobblers of the place by the
assmbly of Two Thousand of Vijaya-Vātāpi, who are described as well-versed in the
great four-fold lore.

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(B.K. No. 234 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the hill-side to the left of the way leading up to the Taṭṭu-Kōṭi Māruti temple.

This contains a description of Kappe-Arabhaṭṭa whose eminent personality and
excellent virtues are praised in verse. It is the earliest instance of a composition in
the Tripadi metre found in Kannaḍa literature.
Published in Indian Antiquary, Vol. X, pp. 61 ff., No. LXXXIV.

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(B.K. No. 274 of 1927-28.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a wall to the left of entrance into the contral shrine of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

It seems to state that the chief Aychapadēva of Abhimānavura had a temple
erected. This is followed by the names of masons who participated in the construc-
tion.

l.ka: nāvidara kuppa [peṟu]guti yagāvi kalukoḍaṁgeyyu mēṇa tā

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(B.K. No. 275 of 1927-28.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

It registers a gift of fifty-one pon-gadyāṇa to Bijēśvara by perggaḍe Poleyachchi
of Mahādēvigēri.

l.ka: © svasti śrī mahādēvigēriya pe¨rggaḍe poleyacci bi-

group.h3:
No. 454-459.
(B.K. Nos. 276 to 281 of 1927-28.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
The first, second and fifth of these six inscriptions refer to the carvings of sculp-
tures. The third describes Māṇi of Bādāvi. The next one is the citation of a
Sanskrit verse and the last seems to be the name of a person.

inscription.head: No. 454.
inscription.p: On the east wall of Pāpanāśēśvara temple.

inscription.head: No. 455.
inscription.p: On a pillar at the eastern entrance of the same temple.

inscription.head: No. 456.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the same temple.

inscription.head: No. 457.
inscription.p: On a pillar to the right of the eastern entrance of the same temple.

inscription.head: No. 458.
inscription.p:
On a pillar to the right of the southern entrance into the mukha-maṇḍapa of the
Virūpāksha temple.

inscription.head: No. 459.
inscription.p:
On the back of the pillar to the left of the eastern entrance.
into the same maṇḍapa.

group.h3:
No. 460-461.
(B.K. Nos. 284 and 285 of 1927-28.)
MAHĀKŪṬA, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
The first is a brief record probably referring to the mason who constructed the
pillar. The second is the citation of a Sanskrit verse purporting to state that sin
committed in a holy place cannot be atoned for. The stanza, however, is engraved
defectively.

inscription.head: No. 460.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Mahākūṭēśwara temple.

inscription.head: No. 461.
inscription.p: On another pillar in the same maṇḍapa.

l.note: The intendedvinaśyati.

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(B.K. No. 287 of 1927-28.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a wall in the temple near Sauḷubhāvi.

It seems to register some gift in connection with the performance of gō-sahasra by
Duggaṭi-avasidda.

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(B.K. No. 289 of 1927-28.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a beam in the Gauḍara-guḍi.

It records a gift to the goddess Durggā Bhagavatī by the Five-Hundred Mahā-
janas, Eight Nagaras, Three-Hundred and Twenty Urāḷis.

l.ka:
svasti śrīmadā¨ryyāpuradā śrī mahācatu¨rvvidyasamudayamaynūṟuṁ mahājana
mu[ṁ] ·eṇṭunnagaramuṁ nūṟu ·i¨rppadinvaru

group.h3:
No. 464-471.
(B.K. No. 3 to 10 of 1928-29.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
Most of these short inscriptions seem to mention the names of individuals who were
associated with the temples under reference, either as donors of the particular parts,
or as masons who shaped them or as pilgrims.

inscription.head: No. 464.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Durgā temple.

inscription.head: No. 465.
inscription.p: On another pillar in the same temple. (In archaic Nāgari characters).

inscription.head: No. 466.
inscription.p: In the mukha-maṇḍapa of the same temple.

inscription.head: No. 467.
inscription.p: On a wall in the same temple.

inscription.head: No. 468.
inscription.p: On another wall.

inscription.head: No. 469.
inscription.p: On the stone-gate leading to the mukha-maṇḍapa of the same temple.

inscription.head: No. 470.
inscription.p: On a stone beam in the Huchchimalli-guḍi.

inscription.head: No. 471.
inscription.p: On the wall right of entrance of the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1928-29.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up near the well at the Huchchimalli-guḍi.
It mentions Bagudēva. The purport of the record is not clear, as it is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(B.K. No. 133 of 1928-29.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar to the proper left of the entrance into the Mālegitti Śivālaya on a
boulder behind the Taluk office.

It states that the magnificent edifice (i.e., temple) was the creation of
Āryamiñchi Upādhyāya, who was evidently a mason.

group.h3:
No. 474-477.
(B.K. Nos. 135 to 138 of 1928-29.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
Near an image of Gaṇēśa on a rock in a place known as śībāra
on the way to Mahākūṭa.

These short inscriptions seem to refer to the carving of figures on the rock and
the names of persons who were associated with the work.

l.ka: nāka tārasa kada pravare tuṁgaśi • • • ḷi

group.h3:
No. 478-479.
(B.K. Nos. 155 and 156 of 1928-29.)
MAHĀKŪṬA, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p: These two short inscriptions seem to refer to individual names or epithets.

inscription.head: No. 478.
inscription.p: On a pillar in a shrine to the north of the pond.

inscription.head: No. 479.
inscription.p: On a pillar lying in the north bank of the same pond.

inscription.head:
No. 480.
(B.K. No. 164 of 1928-29.)
AGARAHĀḶ, BADAṀI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Nāganātha temple at Nāganāthanakoḷḷa.
It seems to mention triraṅga pillar.

group.h3:
No. 481-486.
(B.K. Nos. 167 to 172 of 1928-29.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
These short inscriptions some of which are damaged, contain individual names or
epithets and references to some constructions.
In a balcony on the south face of the Pāpanāśēśvara temple.

inscription.head: No. 484.
inscription.p: At the top of an image above a window in the south wall.

edition.p: On the north wall of the maṇḍapa of the same temple.

group.h3:
No. 487-488.
(B.K. Nos. 174 and 175 of 1928-29.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
The first inscription states that the image of Śiva was carved out by the mason
Pullappa bearing the epithet Nirmāṇadēva. The second refers to Sarvasiddhi-
āchārya as the mason responsible for the southern section of the temple.

inscription.head: No. 487.
inscription.p: Above an image of Śiva on the north wall of the Virūpāksha temple.

inscription.head: No. 488.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Basavaṇṇa shrine in the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 489.
(B.K. No. 176 of 1928-29.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar to the left of the entrance into the Mallikārjuna temple.

This inscription is engraved in archaic characters of about the eighth century.
It is of the nature of a label and seems to indicate that the pillar on which it is engraved
was a gift made in the name of Mahārāja Taila who was a Pergaḍe (local officer). It
is not unlikely that this Taila is identical with Taila I, a later member of the Chālukyas
of Bādāmi who were reduced to a state of subordination with the ascendancy of the
Rāshṭrakūṭas. It is significant to note that Taila is also described as Mahādhirājā.

inscription.head:
No. 490.
(B.K. No. 4 of 1929-30.)
BĀSARKŌḌ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone standing in a field near the village.
It seems to eulogise a person who was the author of some construction.

l.note: sa appears to have been faintly engraved above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 491.
(B.K. No. 179 of 1926-27.)
RŌṆ, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying close to the Basavaṇṇa temple.

It records the gift of bani to the Māhajanas of Rōṇa by Turagaveḍeṅga of Bāli-
vaṁśa, bearing the hereditary title Kishkindhāpuravarēśvara.
Published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XIII, pp. 185 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1927-28.)
KARMAḌI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the front wall of the Kalamēśvara temple.

The expression Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti and Sunday only are preserved in the date
portion, the other details being lost.

It records a gift of land to the temple of Īśānarāśi-bhaṭāra, by the Mahājanas of
Kāremaḍi. A portion of the gift land was to be utilised for imparting education.

inscription.head:
No. 493.
(B.K. No. 64 of 1927-28.)
HAITĀPURA, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar built into the wall of the Māruti temple.

It states that Bhagibe, wife of Bijja of the Chālukya lineage had a tank built
below a hill, between Kōvuṟūr and Mōṟivaḷavu. The record describes the Chālukya
family as belonging to the Brahma-Kshatriya community and gives the genealogy of
Bijja as follows:—Baddiyamma, his son Maḍidāra, his son Gōkaṇa, his son Kattiyara,
his son Gōvaṇṇa, his son Rāja, his son Bijja.

l.note: The

inscription.head:
No. 494.
(B.K. No. 258 of 1927-28.)
HAḶḶŪR, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the temple called Mēlguḍi.
It refers to Tṛi(Tri)mushṭidēva, probably a deity.

inscription.head:
No. 495.
(B.K. No. 288 of 1927-28.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Lāḍkhān temple.
It seems to refer to a gift made by Dēvakutāḷa. The epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 496.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1928-29.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Jaina-Nārāyaṇa temple.
It contains a praise of the preceptor Gaṇḍavimukta-Bhaṭṭāraka.

group.h3:
No. 497-508.
(B.K. Nos. 20 to 31 of 1928-29.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
These short inscriptions seem to mention the names of persons who were indivi-
dually associated with the construction of the merlons of the fort wall. The first one
is descriptive.

inscription.head: No. 497.
inscription.p: On a wall in the Lāḍkhān temple.

edition.p: On the fort wall.

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1928-29.)
VAJHĀḶ, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in Survey No. 27.

This inscription seems to register certain regulations regarding the distribution of
land in favour of the Gāvuṇḍas. It mentions Kisukāḍu. The epigraph is fragmentary.

l.note: The

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(B.K. No. 165 of 1928-29.)
NĀGARAHĀḶ, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Nāganātha temple at Nāganāthana-koḷḷa.

It seems to refer to some construction by Uṟu, Avuga and Chanda. The epigraph
is partly worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(B.K. No. 168 of 1928-29.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In a balcony on the south face of the Pāpanāśēśvara temple.
It refers to a teacher and devotion to god. The record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 512.
(B.K. No. 169 of 1928-29.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In a balcony on the south face of the Pāpanāśēśvara temple.

It refers to the erection of an image by a person whose name is not fully pre-
served.

inscription.head:
No. 513.
(B.K. No. 283 of 1927-28.)
PAṬṬADAKAL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Virūpāksha temple.

The following names are engraved below the scenes from the story of the
Mahābhārata sculptured on the pillar. The damaged names are omitted.

inscription.head:
No. 514.
(B.K. No. 9 of 1927-28.)
BEḶVAṆIKI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone lying close to the Kalamēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 865 corresponding to 943-44 A.D. It records the death of a
warrior.

inscription.head:
No. 515.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1928-29.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone pillar set up in front of the Huchchimalli-guḍi.

This is dated Śaka 867, Viśvāvasu, corresponding to 945-46 A.D. It records the
performance of gōsahasra by a person named Keychaya.

inscription.head:
No. 516.
(B.K. No. 89 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the dhvajastambha in front of the Nārāyaṇadēva temple.

This is dated Śaka 8[9]9, Īśvara, Jyēshṭha puṇṇami, Monday, Rōhiṇī-nakshatra
corresponding to 977 A.D., June 4. The nakscatra, however, was Mūlā.
It records the erection of a mānastambha. The details are lost.

l.note: The rest of the

inscription.head:
No. 517.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab on the tank bund.

It states that the record was composed by Sāmiyaṇṇa and the inscribed slab was
set up by Chāvuṇḍa. Only the last few lines of the epigraph are preserved, the earlier
portion having been broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 518.
(B.K. No. 117 of 1926-27.)
HOSŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone near the local mosque.
It seems to record the death of a warrior in a fight.

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(B.K. No. 217 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the top of the cavern called Pañchaliṅgapaḍi near Cave No. IV.

It praises Mādidē[va], a devotee of the god Sōmēśvara and mentions Agastya-
Pushpagiri. The record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 520.
(B.K. No. 238 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar inside the Bhūtanātha temple.
It states that the lamp-post was a gift of Kariya Sōḍiyamma.

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(B.K. No. 259 of 1927-28.)
HAḶḶŪR, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a wall of the room on the way leading up the roof of the temple called Mēlguḍi.
It is an incomplete passage describing the virtues of a person.

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(B.K. No. 145 of 1928-29.)
HAṄGARGI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the platform of the house of Sivaputri Govindappa Ganiger.

It records the regulations laid down by Mahāsāmanta Daśavarmma in respect of
the administration of the tract of Peṅgarage Twelve, such as concessions in land assess-
ment and collection of fines for offences.

inscription.head:
No. 523.
(B.K. No. 83 of 1929-30.)
BĀGĒVĀḌI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the south wall of the house of Viṭhal Singh Rajput.

The epigraph in Nāgarī characters reads Dhārādhari which may be a personal
same.

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(B.K. No. 72 of 1929-30.)
TUMBIGI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built in the left side of the northern gateway of the village.

This is dated Śaka 936, Ānanda, Mārggaśira-Amāvāsye, Tuesday, Vriśchika-
sañkrānti. On the said tithi occurred Dhanus-sankrānti, when the weekday was
Thursday. If, however, the month was Kārtika, the details would regularly corres-
pond to 1014 A.D., October 26.

It records a gift of land to the Bāṇarāsi-maṭha by Nāgadēvayya, the Uroḍeya
of the agrahāra of Tuṁbige in Pagalaṭṭi-Three Hundred. The gift was entrusted
into the hands of the Hundred Mahājanas.

l.ka: • yasya yasya yadā bhūmi tasya tasya
tadā paḷaṁ svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harē-

inscription.head:
No. 525.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1928-29.)
KANDGAL, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab set up in the compound of the Hanumān temple.
This is dated Śaka 936, Ānanda, corresponding to 1014-15 A.D.

It records a deed of settlement (saṁsthā-patra) made in favour of the Ūroḍeyas
and the Gāvuṇḍas of Kandagale by the chief Gōyindapayyarasa.

inscription.head:
No. 526.
(B.K. No. 136 of 1926-27.)
HUYILGŌḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in front of the Rāmaliṅgadēva temple.

This is dated Śaka 938, Rākshasa, Māgha śu. 5, Sunday. This may be equated
to 1016 A.D., January 17, the week-day being Thursday. The Śaka year was
current.

It records the death in a fight of a gorava who seems to have encountered the
enemy at the instance of the Two-Hundred. It mentions Poṁbuchcha. The inscrip-
tion is damaged.

l.ka: svasti sakava¨rṣa 938 neya rākṣasa saṁvatsara māgha
niṟisidōṁ || jitēna labhyatē lakṣmī¨rmṛtēnāpi surāṁganā kṣaṇa vidhvaṁ-
si[ni] kāyē kā ciṁtā maraṇē raṇē

inscription.head:
No. 527.
(B.K. No. 44 of 1929-30.)
MAṬHKAL-DĒVANAHAḶḶI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up before the Rāmaliṅga temple.

This is dated Śaka 943, Dundubhi, Paushya ba. 14, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrā-
nti. The date is irregular. In Dundubi corresponding to Śaka 944 1022 A.D.,
the śaṁkrānti occurred on December 24, Monday, the tithi being Pausha ba. 13.
In the previous year, Durmati, Śaka 943=1021 A.D., the Saṁkrānti occurred on DeceM-
ber 24, Sunday, the tithi being Pausha ba. 2.

It records a gift of land and house-site to the god Kalidēva of Sāvaḷigi by
Tantrapāla Veṇṇamayya-nāyaka.

l.ka: sya tasya tedā phalaṁ || svadattaṁ para-

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1927-28.)
BARDŪR, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Bharatēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 954, Āṅgirasa, Āshāḍha śu. 1, Sunday, corresponding to
1032, A.D., June 11.

It records a gift of land to the god Bharatēśvara. The gift land was purchased
from the Two-Hundred Mahājanas of Bharatāpura and was entrusted into their hands
for protection.

l.note:
Above this
pression mahājana.

inscription.head:
No. 529.
(B.K. No. 218 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the top of the cavern called Pañchaliṅgapaḍi, near Cave No. IV.

This is dated Śaka 955, Śrīmukha, Āsvija śu. 5, Sunday, corresponding to 1033
A.D., September 1. The weekday, however, was Saturday.

It states that Perggaḍe Kamchiya Chāvayya, a subordinate of Nelagōṭemalla
Kommarāja, had the image of Mallikārjuna consecrated on the southern side of
the Bhūtēśvara temple at Agastya Pushpagiri.

l.ka: ṣṭhayanuṁ māḍisidaṁ [||] pāpanāśamappa tī¨rtthaṁ viṣṇupādaṁ rudrapā-

inscription.head:
No. 530.
(B.K. No. 239 of 1928-29.)
MORAB, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Hanumantadēva temple.

This is dated Śaka 981, Vikāri, Kāttika śu. 2, Wednesday, Pūrvāshāḍhā-nakshatra.
The details correspond to 1059 A.D. November 10, f.d.n. °50, if the month is Mārggaśira.

It records the death by Sanyasana-vidhi of the Jaina teacher Nāgachandra-
Siddhāntadēva, in the Ghaṭāntakiya-basadi at Moramba. Nāgachandra who is
praised as a great scholar and advocate of the Jaina doctrine, was the disciple of Jaya-
kīrtti and belonged to Kārēya-gaṇa and Mailāpa-tīrtha of Yānnīya-sangha. The
nisidige memorial bearing the epigraph was set up by his disciple Kanakaśakti
Siddhāntadēva. The epigraph is composed both in Sanskrit and Kannaḍa verse,
supplemented by prose.

l.note:
The following is engraved on the top of the slab to the left.
śrīmannā-
gacaṁdra
siddāntadēvaṁ [|*]

inscription.head:
No. 531.
(B.K. No. 242 of 1927-28.)
SHIRŪR, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Siddhēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 990, Kīlaka. The other details are not given. The Śaka
with the cyclic year corresponds to 1068-69 A.D.

It records a gift of land to the god Siddhadēva of Karṇana Sirivura, by Dēva-
rāyanāyaka of Nimba-kula, the Prathu of Kollāpura. It mentions Gaurakabbe,
a devotee of the goddess Mahālakshmī of Kollāpura. In a lengthy praśasti
associated with this goddess, She is described as Siṁhavāhane (i.e., seated on a lion),
Rudrārdhāṅgōttuṅganivāsinī (i.e., dwelling higher up in the half body of Rudra)
and supreme among the deities of the Mahātīrtha and Ādipīṭha, (i.e., foremost Śakti-
pīṭha), surrounded by seven hundred tīrthas headed by Śrī-Vīśālā, and the assembly
of sixty-four Yōginīs and Siddhas.

inscription.head:
No. 532.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1930-31.)
MANAGŌḶI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a fragment built into the wall (left of entrance) of the maṇḍapa of the
Hanumān temple.

This is dated Śaka 993, Virōdhikṛit, Pushya śu. 1, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁ-
krānti, corresponding to 1071 A.D., December 25.

It records a gift of land to the god Bhōgēśvara, out of a grant made earlier. The
record is much damaged.

edition.p: Beginning lost.

l.ka: [vutta]rāyaṇa saṁkrāṁnti pa¨rbba nimitya munna mūliga ca[nda]gā • • • • •
mānya 300 mattaroḷage bhōgēśvara dēva¨rgge biṭṭa 110 matta • • •

inscription.head:
No. 533.
(B.K. No. 44 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the wall of the Mallikārjuna temple.

This is dated Śaka 998, Naḷa, Saṅkrānti, Sunday. In the said cyclic year corres-
ponding to 1076 A.D., Tulā-saṁkrānti occurred on Sunday, in which case the date
corresponds to 1076 A.D., September 25.

It records a gift to the god Kalidēva by Saṁkarakōṭi, disciple of Dēvēndra-
paṇḍita. Rūvāri Kētōja engraved the record.

l.note: After the9 there is a dot and after 8 a stroke, which evidently are to be ignored.

inscription.head:
No. 534.
(B.K. No. 81 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a niche in a well called Pallēdavara-bhāvī.
It states that the well was constructed by Dharmaseṭṭi, disciple of Pavitrarāsi.

inscription.head:
No. 535.
(B.K. No. 246 of 1927-28.)
SHIRŪR, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up behind the Rāmalingasvāmin temple.

It mentions a chief of the Telugu Chōḻa family bearing the titles Uṟeyūr-pura
paramēśvara and Iḻ[ivi]ṭṭi-paṟeghōshaṇa. The emblem of the family was lion and
their banner had the figure of a tiger on it. The epigraph is fragmentary and
damaged.

l.note: The beginning is lost.
The continuation is completely damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 536.
(B.K. No. 92 of 1928-29.)
MAṆṬŪR, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone standing near the temple of Rāmaliṅga.
It records the death of a warrior in a fight. The inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 537.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1929-30.)
DHAVAḶGI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone standing before the Hanumān temple.

It seems to record the gift of a well. It mentions Iṅgaḷēśvara. The epigraph is
damaged.

l.note: The beginning is

inscription.head:
No. 538.
(B.K. No. 27 of 1929-30.)
HULLŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab standing near the entrance into the village.

It seems to describe the valour of a chief named Allapuli. It mentions Barma
The inscription is damaged and fragmentary.

l.note: The beginning is lost.
The continuation is missing.

inscription.head:
No. 539.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1929-30.)
HULLŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone standing in a field.

It records a gift of land to a Jaina temple of Kaṇḍūru-gaṇa, by Mṛigāvatiyabbe
in the presence of the Twenty-four Ūroḍeyas and Pañchamaṭha-sthāna of Puluvuraṇi.

inscription.head:
No. 540.
(B.K. No. 34 of 1929-30.)
KŌḶŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying near the tank close to the village.

It seems to record a gift of money. It mentions two persons named Maṁjarasa
and Lōkarasa. The inscription is damaged.

edition.h4: Second Side The first side is completely worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 541.
(B.K. No. 38 of 1929-30.)
KUṆṬŌJI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a mutilated slab built into the south wall of the Īśvara temple.

The extant portion of the inscription contains details regarding the boundaries
of the gift-land and the imprecation. In the former context Bidirkkunde is mentioned.
The main part of the epigraph is missing.

inscription.head:
No. 542.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1928-29.)
KOṆṆŪR, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken stone built into a wall of Bīradēva temple.

The available portion of the date mentions the cyclic year Śukla. This may be
equated to 1149-50 A.D., as the epigraph can be assigned to the 12th century on
consideration of palaeography.

It records the gift of a house for the maintenance of a free water shed by Rāghava-
ghaḷisāsa of Jaṁbuge. The gift was entrusted into the hands of the Mahājanas of
Kōṇiganūru. The inscription is damaged.

l.note: The

l.ka: ra sthaḷada brā[hma]ṇaru samasta praje gauṁḍu sa¨rvvamānyavāgi

inscription.head:
No. 543.
(B.K. No. 253 of 1927-28.)
ANAGAVĀḌI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in front of the Duggavva temple.

This is dated Śrīmukha, Āshāḍha ba. 5, Monday. Palaeographically the record
may be assigned to the 12th century A.D. The said cyclic year may therefore be
taken to correspond to 1153-54 A.D., when the other details correspond to July 12,
the weekday being Sunday.

It records a gift of land to a Jaina temple. The epigraph is damaged and the
details are lost.

l.ka: • vadalli ma 2 baḍagaṇa holadalli ma 4 ma • • •

inscription.head:
No. 544.
(B.K. No. 18 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the well called Kallappana-bhāvi at the old ruined village.

This is dated Pramādi, Māgha ba. 6, Sitavāra. The record may be assigned
to the 12th century when the details correspond to 1160 A.D., January 29, Friday
f.d.t. °68.

It records the death of Śānti-paṇḍita who had built the shrine of Mallinātha
at Iṅgaḷēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 545.
(B.K. No. 110 of 1929-30.)
MUTTAGI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab standing in the Siddharāmayya temple.

This inscription is engraved in continuation of No. 24 above (B.K. No. 109
1929-30), and bears the date, Pārthiva, Āshāḍha, ba. 10, Bṛihavāra, (i.e., Saturday),
Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkrānti. As the earlier inscription is dated in 1142 A.D., the said
cyclic year coming immediately after it would fall in 1165 A.D. The details, however,
do not work out correctly for this year. In 1165, A.D., the above tithi corresponds
to July 4, Sunday, but the said Saṅkrānti occurred on Āshāḍha ba. 1 or June 26,
Saturday.

It records the remission of the tax baṇṇigedeṟe on the garden of the Prasanna-
kēśava temple, by Hammarasa-daṇḍanāyaka, the adhikāri of Tarddavāḍi Thousand
and the Karaṇas as paramēśvaradatti. A contract of araṇi-guttige of the garden was
effected by the Mahājanas of the Brahmapuri with Hoṭṭeya Bammaṇa who was to
supply garlands to the deity every day. Further gifts to the same deity by several
individuals including the members of the trading guilds are also registered.

inscription.head:
No. 546.
(B.K. No. 98 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab on the brink of a well near the Siddhēśvara temple.

This is dated Krōdhi, Vaisākha śu. 8, Thursday. The palaeography of the
epigraph being of about the 12th century, the date may be equated to 1184 A.D.,
April 19, Thursday, f.d.t. °07.
It records the death of Chindiya Chandaṇṇa of Muḷugunda in a fight.

l.ka: śrī krōddhi vaisākha suddha 8 miya guruvāradalu muḷuguṁ-
dada ciṁdiya ceṁdaṁṇanu raṇadalu maḍida maṁgaḷa

inscription.head:
No. 547.
(B.K. No. 3 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the prākāra of the Trikūṭēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1107, Krōdhi, the other details being lost. The date corres-
ponds to 1184-85 A.D., the Śaka year being current.

It records a gift to the god Trikūṭēśvara. It mentions Ereguppe in Huligeṟe
Three Hundred. Mention is also made of Hermāḍirāya, probably of the Chālukya
family and Chandrabhūshaṇamuni. The record is fragmentary and damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 548.
(B.K. No. 269 of 1927-28.)
TUḶASIGĒRI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Māruti temple.
This is dated Śaka 1107, Viśvāvasu, Saṁkramaṇa, the other details being lost.

It records a gift of land made by Jagaddāni Jagatāpa Amme-Sāvanta to a Śiva
temple, for repairs and worship. Sōma-sāvanta is mentioned in the earlier portion
which is much damaged.

l.note: The rest of the inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 549.
(B.K. No. 33 of 1930-31.)
SATIHĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone converted into a mortar in the house of Saṇṇappa-Gauḍa.

This is dated the cyclic year Pramādīcha, Jyēshṭha-puṇṇami, Monday, lunar
eclipse. As the record can be assigned to the 12th century, the date might be
1193 A.D., May 17, when the given details correspond regularly.

It seems to register gifts of land to the gods Kannēśvara, Āchēśvara and Āṟeya-
śaṅkaradēva. The inscription is damaged.

l.note: The rest of the

inscription.head:
No. 550.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the well at the entrance to the village.

This is dated Śaka 1117, Ānanda, Chaitra ba. 2, Saturday, corresponding to 1194
A.D., April 9. The Śaka year was current.

It records the death of the grand-mother of Peṇḍara Bāchi, who was a devotee
of the god Tīrthachandraprabha.

inscription.head:
No. 551.
(B.K. No. 436 of 1926.)
BĀḌA, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab lying near the Śiva temple.

The last four lines record gifts to the deities Śiva and Kēśava of Bāḍa. The top
portion is lost. On palaeographical grounds the inscription may be assigned to the 12th
century A.D.

l.note: The beginning of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 552.
(B.K. No. 446 of 1926.)
TAḌKŌḌ, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a niche to the proper right of the entrance into the central shrine of the Kala-
mēśvara temple.

It is an invocation in Sanskrit verse to Chandranātha-jina for the protection of the
teacher Vāsupūjya, disciple of Bāḷachandra, of Yāpanīya-saṅgha, Svarṇapāshāṇa-
gachchha and Vṛikshamūla-gaṇa.

l.ka:
[ca]ṁdraṁ sucaritacaritaṁ bāḷacaṁdrātmajātaṁ pūjyaṁ śrīvāsupūjyabratipatimavatā[ṁ]
caṁdravāthō jinēṁdraḥ ||

inscription.head:
No. 553.
(B.K. No. 457 of 1926.)
MUGAD, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up on the tank bund.

It seems to record the death of Nāga-gāvuṇḍa in a fight on the occasion of a cattle
raid. The inscription is damaged and worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 554.
(B.K. No. 462 of 1926.)
SIDDHĀPUR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of the image of Bhagavatī in the Śiva-maṇḍapa.

It states that the image of the goddess Bhagavatī of the god Mūlasthāna was
caused to be made by Sāgalara Kaṁmaṟa-seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 555.
(B.K. No. 463 of 1926.)
SIDDHĀPUR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of Gaṇēśa image in the Śiva-maṇḍapa.

It refers to the construction of the image of Vināyaka. The inscription is damaged
and the details are lost.

l.ka: di vināyakana māḍisida maṁgaḷa mahā śrī

inscription.head:
No. 556.
(B.K. No. 31 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of the image of Nēminātha in the Jain basti.

It states that the image was the gift of Saṁkhadēva of Mūla-saṁgha and Dēva-
gaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 557.
(B.K. No. 34 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the vacant site opposite to Nagarēśvara temple.

It states that the inscription is set up to indicate the boundary of the site of the
dāna-s11ālā, made over to the teacher, Tribhuvanatilaka-Śāntinātha of Vasudhaika-
Bāndhava-Jinālaya, by the One Thousand.

inscription.head:
No. 558.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the front wall of the Liṅgāyata-maṭha.
It records a gift of two shops to the god Nannēśvara by the One Thousand.

inscription.head:
No. 559.
(B.K. No. 38 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a beam of the Chandramauḷīśvara temple.

It records a gift of betel nuts to a god on the occasion of Dīpāvaḷi by Kēṇikāṟa
Echiseṭṭi, described as the son of the One Thousand of Lokkiguṇḍi which is said to
have been an agrahāra gifted by Rāma.

inscription.head:
No. 560.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Māṇikyēśvara temple.

It refers to the bhōga-maṇḍapa constructed on a well by the teacher Kriyāśakti-
paṇḍita and mentions Baḍḍiyara Basaviseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 561.
(B.K. No. 47 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the base of pillar in the Nannēśvara temple.

It seems to record the setting up of a pillar for a deity by a lady. The epigraph
is worn out and the details are lost.

l.ka:
svasti samasta • • • guṇasaṁpaṁna dānavinōda yo[sva]rapādapaṁkajabhramareyappa

inscription.head:
No. 562.
(B.K. No. 139 of 1926-27.)
HUYILGŌḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone built into the platform of the Māruti temple.

It seems to record the death of Mārasiṁgha, son of Rēvayya, in a defensive fight
at the instance of the Hundred and Twenty of Puligoḷa. The epigraph is damaged.

l.ka: paridu muṭṭi yi[ṟi]du mundāda neraviyanārpa[ḍedo] • • • • • • • • • • valōḍi

inscription.head:
No. 563.
(B.K. No. 165 of 1926-27.)
KARDI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab set up in front of the chāvaḍi.

It records a gift of land to the god Kēśava by Chāvuṇḍa-gāvuṇḍa and Rēva-
gāvuṇḍa. The epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 564.
(B.K. No. 167 of 1926-27.)
KARDI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the chāvaḍi.

The extant portion gives the cyclic year Dundubhi, Kārttika ba. 14, the details
being insufficient for verification.

It refers to a lady disciple (name lost) of the Jaina monk Harishēṇa of Mūla-
saṅgha and Sēna-gaṇa. The epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 565.
(B.K. No. 180 of 1926-27.)
RŌṆ, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone in front of the Vīrabhadra temple.

The only details of the date available are Māgha, śu. 11. It mentions a Sāhaṇi.
The inscription is badly worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 566.
(B.K. No. 209 of 1926-27.)
NIḌGUNDI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Kañchēśvara temple.

It states that an oil mill of the god Akkabēśvara of Niḍugundi was placed in charge
of Māhēśvara Kāḷiseṭṭi by Īśānyaśakti, the āchārya of the temple. The stipulation
seems to be that the donee and the successive members of his family were to supply
a fixed quantity of oil to the god.

inscription.head:
No. 567.
(B.K. No. 43 of 1927-28.)
NĀGARAHAḶḶI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the pedestal of a saptamātṛikā stone in the ruined Pañchaliṅga temple on the
bank of the Tuṅgabhadrā.

It seems to record some gift of Tribhuvana-Nārāyaṇa, son of Śāgarayya, the
Maḻvāraseṭṭi of Maḷale-nāḍu. The inscription is fragmentary.

l.ka:
svasti śrīmaddē • vēḷapuraparamēśvara kāṁsyapagōtra maḷalenāḍa ma[ḻvā]raseṭṭi
śāgarayyana saṁyibbeya magaṁ tribhuvananārāyaṇadha¨rmma sē •

inscription.head:
No. 568.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1927-1928.)
KALKĒRI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pedestal of a lost image preserved in the village chāvaḍi.

It records the construction of a Jaina temple at Kalikeṟe and the installation of
the image of Pārśvanātha therein for the preceptor Sakaḷachandra-bhaṭṭāraka by Hāli
gāvuṇḍa, disciple of Bhānukīrtti-Siddhāntidēva of Mūla-saṁgha, Koṇḍakund-
ānvaya, Krāṇūr-gaṇa and Tintriṇī-gachchha.

l.ka: drabhaṭṭārakadēva¨rgge basadiyaṁ māḍisi koṭṭu pā¨rśvanātha[dē]vara pra-
tiṣṭhayaṁ māḍida || maṁgaḷa śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 569.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1927-28.)
ḌAMBAḶ, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the platform of the maṇḍapa of the temple called Ḍabbaguḍi.
It refers to the gaḍimba of the land belonging to the god Mādhavēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 570.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1927-28.)
ḌAMBAḶ, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar at the left of entrance into the Kalamēśvara temple.

It describes Māsavāḍi-nāḍu which is stated to have been situated on the northern
bank of the river Dakshiṇa-Gaṅgā (i.e., Tuṅgabhadrā), in Kuntaḷa-vishaya. It then
states that Permāḍi secured from his overlord the rulership of Māsavāḍi, by dint of his
valour. Permāḍi had two sons Harihara and Kuppa and the latter was the father of
Harihara and Mallidēva. The record is mutilated and incomplete.

l.note: The record stop here.

inscription.head:
No. 571.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1927-28.)
ḌAMBAḶ, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near a well in Survey No. 413.

It enjoins that no taḻāra should take any produce of the garden, evidently referring
to the field bearing the inscription originally.

inscription.head:
No. 572.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1927-28.)
DŌṆĪ, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the chāvaḍi.

The record, engraved in continuation of B.K. No. 74 of 1927-28 (published in the
Bombay Karnatak Inscriptions, Vol. I, Part II, No. 140, pp. 169 ff) contains two
incomplete dates: (1) 13, Sunday and (2) Pushya śu. 15, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁ-
kramaṇa. These cannot be verified.

It seems to register gifts of land, shops and house-sites by the Mahājanas of
Drōṇīpura, to the basadi built by Sōviseṭṭi. The supplement, engraved on the top
of the slab, records gifts of oil-mill and betel nut.
