inscription.head:
No. 573.
(B.K. No. 237 of 1927-28.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in front of Yusuf-sahib's house.

This commences with an invocation to the Varāha incarnation. It seems to
describe an eminent personage. The inscription being badly damaged, its purport
cannot be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 574.
(B.K. No. 273 of 1927-28.)
BANAŚAṄKARI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar lying in the temple of Banaśaṅkari.

It states that Kēti, son of Sāyama, established charities such as free feeding-
house and educational facilities in places like Savasi, Maḷeyakhēḍa and Naṟugunda,
so that religious merit might accrue to him.

l.ka: ḷa naṟugundaṁgaḷoḷtanna puṇyaṁ sale kēti mā-

inscription.head:
No. 575.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1928-29.)
KANDGAL, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fragment of a stone near the Īśvara temple.

It seems to record a gift of land, oil, etc., made in favour of a deity and a house-site
to the sthānapati Sarvvēśvara-paṇḍita. The record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 576.
(B.K. No. 85 of 1928-29.)
KOṆṆŪR, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fragmentary hero-stone lying in the Hanumān temple.

It seems to record a gift by the Mahājanas headed by the Gāvuṇḍa. The inscrip-
tion is fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 577.
(B.K. No. 89 of 1928-29.)
KOṆṆŪR, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fragmentary stone lying in the Hanumān temple.

It seems to have contained a date in the Chālukya-Vikrama era. It mentions
a nāyaka. The record is fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 578.
(B.K. No. 90 of 1928-29.)
KUNDARGI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone standing near the village chāvaḍi.

This is dated the cyclic year Vikrama, Śrāvaṇa śu., the tithi being lost. It men-
tions a Chauṇḍaya-nāyaka. The epigraph is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 579.
(B.K. No. 98 of 1928-29.)
ANWĀL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the embankment in Survey No. 332.

It records a gift of land for maintaining a water-shed for the use of all beings on
the road to Tineganūru by Sōmaya-Sāvanta of Eṇḍegeṟe, for the merit of his father.
The donor who belonged to the Gutta family is credited with a number of high-sounding
titles, such as Samastabhuvanāśraya. He also bore the epithet Kōgaḷiya-puravar-
ādhis11vara.

inscription.head:
No. 580.
(B.K. No. 158 of 1928-29.)
MAḶGI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken hero-slab set up in the Māruti temple.
This is dated the cyclic year Svabhānu, Āshāḍha śu. 11. It cannot be verified.

It seems to record a gift by the Five Hundred in memory of a hero. The record
is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 581.
(B.K. No. 163 of 1928-29.)
NANDIHĀḶ, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in the Māruti temple.

It seems to register a gift and certain incomes made by Mūka-gāvuṇḍa, the
Mūliga of Gaṟavalūr and the eight Hiṭṭus and others in memory of Haḷḷiya
Basavanta who died in a fight. Refers to Mallidēvarasa as ruling probably the area.
The inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 582.
(B.K. No. 180 of 1928-29.)
VAḌAVAṬṬI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab preserved in the chāvaḍi.
It states that the village of Baḍḍavaṭṭi was a gift to the Baṁka-jinālaya.

inscription.head:
No. 583.
(B.K. No. 181 of 1928-29.)
YARGOPPA, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Hanumantadēva temple.
It records a gift of land to Chōḷasu by Mūliga Malligauṇḍa and Bukaya-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 584.
(B.K. No. 211 of 1928-29.)
HIRĒHĀḶ, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab leaning against the front wall of the Rāmēśvara temple.

It seems to register a gift of land which was entrusted into the hands of Amṛitarāśi-
paṇḍita. The earlier part of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 585.
(B.K. No. 219 of 1928-29.)
BŌGANŪR, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the Nandi pillar in front of the Kalamēśvara temple.

It refers to the setting up of a Nandi pillar and mentions Rājaguru Chandra-
bhūshaṇa as the Sthānāchārya of the god Nagarēśvara of Baṅkāpura which is inciden-
tally described as the capital (rājadhānī) of Indra-vallabha, i.e., the Rāshtrakūṭa
king Indra.

inscription.head:
No. 586.
(B.K. No. 227 of 1928-29.)
HANSI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in front of the Hanumantadēva temple.

It seems to record a gift of land in memory of a hero who fell fighting. The epi-
graph is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 587.
(B.K. No. 230 of 1928-29.)
KOMARGOP, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab set up in the Śiva temple.

It records a gift of money for feeding the Jaina ascetics, associated with the
temple of Tribhuvanatilaka by Perggaḍe Vāsiyaṇṇa's wife Chāmikabbe who was a
disciple of Bāḷachandra Siddhāntadēva.

edition.h4:
TEXT. This inscription is engraved to the proper right of the seated figure
slab.

inscription.head:
No. 588.
(B.K. No. 236 of 1928-29.)
MORAB, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fragment of a slab built into the wall of the Kalamēśvara temple.

It refers to Nīraḻayara Mārasiṁgayya and Sīvaṭada Beḷḷagāvuṇḍa. The record
is fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 589.
(B.K. No. 240 of 1928-29.)
NĀGARAHAḶḶI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of Hanumantadēva temple.
It states that the land was a gift to the god Śāntanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 590.
(B.K. No. 244 of 1928-29.)
SISUVINAHAḶḶI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in the Hanumantadēva temple.
It refers to a cattle raid and mentions the village Sisuhaḻi.

edition.p: Sections I and II are completely damaged.

l.ka: niṟidu tuṟuvaṁ koṇḍu pōge si[su]haṟi baridu[du]

inscription.head:
No. 591.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1929-30.)
GUṆḌAKARJI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a mutilated Jaina image in the Hanumān temple.

It contains the names of the Yakshīs, Aparājitā, Mahāmānasī, Anantamati and
Chakrēśvārī and, the Yakshas, Vṛishabha, Pātāḷa, Kubēra and Śāntinātha of the
Jaina pantheon.

inscription.head:
No. 592.
(B.K. No. 19 of 1929-30.)
HALLŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a mutilated slab built into the front wall of the Hanumān temple.
It mentions a Mahāsāmanta whose name is lost. The record is damaged.

edition.h4: TEXT. The beginning of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 593.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1929-30.)
HANDARGAL, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a large hero-stone standing in the ruined Saṅgamēśvara temple.

This seems to describe the death of a hero in a fight. It seems to mention the
warrior Tantrapāḷa Karṇa. The inscription is much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 594.
(B.K. No. 23 of 1929-30.)
HANDARGAL, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a small hero-stone standing in the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

It mentions Sarvvēśvara, the āchārya of the maṭha belonging to the god Svyaṁbhu
Toṇḍēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 595.
(B.K. No. 36 of 1929-30.)
KŌḶŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone standing under a bilva tree.

It records a gift for heroism, made by the Mummuridaṇḍas and the eight Hiṭṭus
of Kōḷūru and Mahāmaṇḍālēśvara Vīra-Hemmāḍi to Rāvai, son of Nāgaiya, probably in
appreciation of the heroism shown by him.

inscription.head:
No. 596.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1929-30.)
KUṆṬŌJI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a mutilated stone near the well in the Īśvara temple.

It contains details of a series of grants in the course of which reference is made to
Uttarāvaṇa-saṁkrānti, the deity Mēchalēśvara and the measure called Dāna-
chintāmaṇiya-gaḷe. The epigraph is fragmentary and its major portion is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 597.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1929-30.)
TĀḶIKŌṬI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder-stone called Sīme-Vīrappa.

It registers a gift of land to the temple of the god Sahasraliṅga, situated to the
west of Rāmatīrtha. It mentions Koḍeyanūru.

inscription.head:
No. 598.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1929-30.)
TAMMADHAḌḌI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the village chāvaḍi.

It states that the slab was the nishidi memorial set up in honour of Bhōga-
gāvuṇḍa, son of Bammagāvuṇḍa, disciple of Dēvachandra-bhaṭṭāraka, the āchārya
of the Tīrthada Basadi at Iṅgaḷēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 599.
(B.K. No. 116 of 1929-30.)
YARNĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in a field near the Bijapur road.
It states that the land was a gift of Poleyaṇayya.

inscription.head:
No. 600.
(B.K. No. 4 of 1930-31.)
DŌṆŪR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in the Hanumān temple.

It seems to record the death of a hero by name Ārjjahuli whose description is
lost in the damaged portion of the epigraph.

inscription.head:
No. 601.
(B.K. No. 10 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Nārāyaṇadēva temple.

This seems to record a gift of land to the god Gōpāla by Basavarasa and the Prabhus
of Sāḷavāḍige. The inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 602.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a piece of slab built into the north wall of the maṇḍapa in the Sōmanātha temple.
The only details of the date available are Pushya ba. 9, Sunday.

It records a gift of land by the two Five Hundred (Mahājanas) of Iṅgaḷēśvara to
the sthānāchārya Kumāradēva. The earlier portion of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 603.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab set up in the Sōmanātha temple.
It records the gift of a shop probably by the residents of Ūraḍagēri.

inscription.head:
No. 604.
(B.K. No. 19 of 1930-31.)
MANAGŌḶI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fragment built into the wall of the house of Kaḷḷimaṭhada Basayya.

It seems to record a gift of income from cess to a deity. It mentions Mādiyaṇṇa
of Jaṁbuge. The record is damaged and fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 605.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Liṅgāyata-maṭha.

This is dated the cyclic year Āṅgirasa, Chaitra-pāḍiva, Monday. Palaeographi-
cally the record may be assigned to 13th century when the details correspond to 121
A.D., March 5, Monday, f.d.t. °02.

It records the death by samādhi of Sānti-seṭṭi, son of Aggaḷa-seṭṭi and a devout
follower of the Jaina faith.

inscription.head:
No. 606.
(B.K. No. 16 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near Sāhib Mohideen's mosque.

This is dated the cyclic year Yuva, Śrāvaṇa śu. 11, Sthiravāra. On palaeographical
grounds the record may be assigned to the 13th century, in which case the details
correspond to 1215 A.D., August 7. The weekday was Friday.
It records the death by samādhi of the pontiff Māghanandi.

inscription.head:
No. 607.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1928-29.)
BĀḌGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Kēśava temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Svabhānu, Āshāḍha śu. 13, Sōmavāra. Palaeogra-
phically the record may be assigned to the 13th century when the date would corres-
pond to 1223 A.D., June 12, Monday.

It records a gift of oil from the oil-mill of the satra for burning a perpetual lamp
before a god by Chaṭeya-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 608.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1930-31.)
IṄGAḶĒŚVAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the well at the entrance to the village.

This is dated the cyclic year Tāraṇa, Śrāvaṇa-amāvāsye, Sitavāra. As the epigraph
can be assigned to the 13th century, the details may be taken to correspond to 1224
A.D., August 16, Friday.
It records the death of a Jaina devotee named Satyaṇṇa by the rite of Sanyasana.

inscription.head:
No. 609.
(B.K. No. 1 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Trikūṭēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1148, Pārthiva, Vaiśākha śu. 3, Vaḍḍavāra, corresponding to
1225 A.D., April 12. The Śaka year was current.

It records the gift of two golden banners to the god Svayaṁbhu Trikūṭēśvara by
Chaṇḍauvve, the daughter (i.e. disciple) of Sidhānti-Kriyāśakti-paṇḍita and wife of
Mallaya who was the Paṭṭasāhaṇi of Mahāmaṇḍalēs11vara Mallidēva, the Kadamba
faudatory of Hāngal.

l.ka: vaiśākha śuddha 3 vaḍḍanāra rōhiṇī nakṣatra śōbhenayō-
ta dēvara magaḷu pātracūḍāmaṇi caṁḍauvve śrī-

inscription.head:
No. 610.
(B.K. No. 441 of 1926.)
GARAG, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up on the tank bund.

This is dated the cyclic year Vikrita, Śrāvaṇa ba. 14, Bṛihaspativāra. As the
record can be assigned to the 13th century on palaeographic grounds, the details
correspond to 1230 A.D., August 8, Thursday, f.d.t. °11.

It records the death by samādhi of the preceptor Śāntivīra of Kummaḍi-gaṇa
of the Yāpanīya-saṁgha.

l.note: The letterva is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 611.
(B.K. No. 189 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar to the left of the southern entrance (inner side) into the
Amṛitēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1153, Khara, Bhādrapada-Amāvāsyā, Saṁkrānti, Thursday,
corresponding to 1231 A.D., August 28, Thursday, f.d.t. °04, the Saṁkrānti being
Kanyā.

It records the gift of income from the Pannāya tax to the god Svayaṁbhu Amṛitēś-
vara of Rājadhāni-paṭṭaṇa Aṇṇigeṟe, by Āriya Rēviseṭṭi who is styled Koṁkaṇadēśa-
chakravartti (i.e., supreme lord of the Koṅkaṇa country).

A gift to the god Bhairava is mentioned in the supplementary portion engraved
on the second face of the pillar.

inscription.head:
No. 612.
(B.K. No. 248 of 1927-28.)
SHIRŪR, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a wall in the maṇḍapa of the Rāmaliṅgasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 1171, Saumya, Chaitra ba. 8, Monday, corresponding to 1249
A.D., April 7, the weekday being Wednesday. If, however, ba. is a mistake for śu.
the details correspond to 1249 A.D., March 22, Monday f.d.t. °75.

It records a gift of money by Dēki-gāvuṇḍi and Suggi-gāvuṇḍi, the wives of Billa-
haraḷa-gāvuṇḍa, for feeding the ascetics in the maṭha of the god Mūlasthāna once a year,
out of the interest accruing therefrom.

inscription.head:
No. 613.
(B.K. No. 204 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in the Jain Basti.

This is dated Śaka 1189, Prabhava, Chaitra ba. 4, Tuesday, corresponding to 1267
A.D., March 15.

It records the death by Samādhi of Keḷadi-avve, wife of Vīrayya who was the son
of Nōmpiseṭṭi, lay-disciple of Sōmadēvāchārya of Mūla-saṁgha and Koṇḍakund-ānvaya.

inscription.head:
No. 614.
(B.K. No. 81 of 1927-28.)
ḌŌṆI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of the Pārśvanatha image kept in front of the Māruti temple.

This is dated Śaka 1191, Śukla, Chaitra śu. °7, Monday, Mṛigaśīrsha, corres-
ponding to 1269 A.D., March 11.

It records the installation of the image of Pārśvanātha in the basadi of Marudēvi-
Avve of Kāṇūr-gaṇa. The image was a gift by Śrīkaraṇada Boppaṇṇa, grandson of
Sēnabōva Ādaṇṇa. The donor is described as a lay-disciple of the teacher Bāḷa-
chandra. At this time the whole of Karṇāta-maṇḍala is said to have been governed by
Tipparāja.

inscription.head:
No. 615.
(B.K. No. 91 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the inner wall of the Pārśvanātha temple.

This is dated Śaka 1197, Yuva, Vaiśākha ba. 13, Thursday, corresponding to 1275
A.D., April 25.

It records the death, by the vow of Sanyāsa, of Amṛitayya, son of Mahāpradhāna,
Heggaḍe Dēvaṇṇa who was a minister and Bāhattara-niyōgādhipati of Tiḷakarasa of
Saraṭūr.

inscription.head:
No. 616.
(B.K. No. 63 of 1929-30.)
SIRŌḶ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder in a field.

This is dated the cyclic year Chitrabhānu, Āśvija śu. 5, Tuesday. As the record
may be placed, on palaeographical grounds, in the 13th century, the details may
correspond to 1282 A.D., September 8.

It records a gift of land to a Jaina temple by the Prabhu and other residents
of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 617.
(B.K. No. 179 of 1928-29.)
UMTAR, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone in the ruined temple.

This is dated Śaka 1208, Pārthiva, Chaitra śu. 11, Sunday, corresponding to 1285
A.D., March 18. The Śaka year was current.

It records a gift of land in Hosavura by Mahāpradhāna Rāvaḷadēva-nāyaka, the
Sarvādhikāri of Toṟagale-nāḍu and Bāgaḍe-nāḍu, at the instance of Sarvādhikāri
Dhāmoi Rāṇe, when Bachai-[nā*]yaka, son of Rājai-nāyaka, was serving as a subordi-
nate official. The gift appears to have been made in memory of a warrior who died
in a fight. These details, however, are not stated clearly in the epigraph.

inscription.head:
No. 618.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1926.)
GARAG, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up on the tank bund.

It seems to state that the field was the property of the Kumuḍi-gaṇa of the
Yāpanīya-saṁgha.

inscription.head:
No. 619.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1926.)
KOṬṬAGI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the face of a pillar in the ruined Basava temple.

It seems to refer to some charitable act by Banaviseṭṭi, a pious devotee of Guru-
liṅga-jaṅgama of the Vīraśaiva faith. As the inscription is written in faulty language, it
is difficult to make out its import.

inscription.head:
No. 620.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Kalyāṇa-maṇḍapa of the Vīranārāyaṇa temple.

It registers a series of gifts of lands, house-sites and money, the purpose of which
cannot be made out. The inscription appears to have been engraved in continuation
of another.

inscription.head:
No. 621.
(B.K. No. 18 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Kalyāṇa-maṇḍapa of the Vīranārāyaṇa temple.
It registers gifts of houses and other objects to the god Traipurusha.

inscription.head:
No. 622.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in front of the Hanumān temple.

It seems to record the death of Haripeya-nāyaka in an expedition against Niḍugal
after the subjugation of four forts, by the son of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tiḷakarasa who
bore the title Magara-murāri and was a devotee of the god Rāmanātha. The epigraph
is damaged.

l.ka: • • verayi nālku du¨rggava koṁḍu niḍugalliṁge dhāḷiye

inscription.head:
No. 623.
(B.K. No. 80 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the ruined Jain basti.

It records the death of Pamigavuḍi, wife of Nāga-gavuṇḍa of Hebbāgi, who was
a disciple of Narēndrasēna-Bhaṭāraka.

inscription.head:
No. 624.
(B.K. No. 143 of 1926-27.)
BAḶAGĀNŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the Virakta-maṭha.

It records gifts of land by Dēparasa, son of mudre-hasta Hemmaya Sāvanta of
Kadaḍi, Uroḍeya and others for the gōsage offerings of the god Sōmaya of Huligeṟe
which was maintained by Sivarāmaya.

inscription.head:
No. 625.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1927-28.)
MĒVUṆḌI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Kenchavva temple.

This record seems to register a gift of land. It mentions Kalukeṟe. It is damaged
and the details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 626.
(B.K. No. 254 of 1927-28.)
ANAGAVĀḌI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in front of the Durgavva temple.

It states that the water level of the river covered one mattar of garden land in
the area called Sēnabōvagere.

inscription.head:
No. 627.
(B.K. No. 255 of 1927-28.)
ANAGAVĀḌI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 17.
It states that the extent of the Sēnabōva's garden was one mattar.

inscription.head:
No. 628.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1928-29.)
DHAVAḶĒŚVARA, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up near the temple of Mēlabasappa.
It states that the land belonged to the god Kharēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 629.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1928-29.)
ANWĀL, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 290.

It seems to record a gift of land to the temple of a Jaina goddess by a Sēnabōva.
The epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 630.
(B.K. No. 148 of 1928-29.)
JALAGĒRI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Jalēśvara temple.
It seems to record a gift to a Mahanta (name lost) by Sōmeya Sāvanta.

inscription.head:
No. 631.
(B.K. No. 159 of 1928-29.)
MAḶGI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying behind the Tāyavva temple.

It records a gift of land as brahmapuri to Brahmānanda Svāmi by Bijeya
Sāhaṇi.

inscription.head:
No. 632.
(B.K. No. 178 of 1928-29.)
UMTAR, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of the Bhairava image in the Śiva temple.
It seems to state that the figure of Bhairava was sculptured by the mason Kalōja.

l.ka: [ṣi]daṁ vistāra • • jana basuroḷu huṭidanu kallasiva tatu bhaktoṁ
nōḍire jagadoḷu māḍisida kṣatige kṣētrādhipati jagadoḷagesaguṁ sa-
leke saduguṇi kalōjage prītiṁdaṁ varavita yīga bhairavatiḷakaṁ || ©

inscription.head:
No. 633.
(B.K. No. 184 of 1928-29.)
YEṆḌIGĒRI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the well of barber Indēśappa's house.
It records a gift of land to the god Billēśvara by Sōmeya Sāvanta.

inscription.head:
No. 634.
(B.K. No. 205 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the temple of Pavāḍi-Basappa.
It seems to mention a person.

inscription.head:
No. 635.
(B.K. No. 213 of 1928-29.)
BAḶḶŪR, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Hanumantadēva temple.
It refers to a person named Bādumayya. The epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 636.
(B.K. No. 220 of 1928-29.)
BYĀLHĀḶ, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Hanumantadēva temple.

It seems to refer to the installation of the god Yādavarāya by Anantarasa in
memory of his father Bommarasa.

edition.h4: TEXT. Same lines in the beginning seem to have been lost.

inscription.head:
No. 637.
(B.K. No. 228 of 1928-29.)
JĀVŪR, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up in front of Hanumantadēva temple.

It records the gift of the village Jāvūru to Sakaḷachandra Bhaṭṭāraka by Bīchiseṭṭi.
It is stated that the village was formerly granted to the Basadi of the goddess Jvāḷā-
māḷinī by a certain • • • • • malladēva. It mentions āchārya Jayakīrtti as the
priest in charge of the religious establishment.

l.note: The continuation is

inscription.head:
No. 638.
(B.K. No. 245 of 1928-29.)
SISUVINAHAḶḶI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in the Hanumantadēva temple.

It records a gift of land to Mallaya-nāyaka by the eight Hiṭṭus and five maṭhas.
It mentions Sesuvinahaḷḷi. The record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 639.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1929-30.)
NALATVĀḌ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone near the Basavaṇṇa temple on the way to Loṭgēri.
It states that the land belonged to the tank.

inscription.head:
No. 640.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1929-30.)
BĀGEVĀḌI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in the Teggina-maṭha.
It seems to state that the land belonged to an individual.

inscription.head:
No. 641.
(B.K. No. 82 of 1929-30.)
BĀGEVĀḌI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone in the Lakkavva-kere field.
It records a gift of land to the god Sōmanātha by Hariyaṇa-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 642.
(B.K. No. 91 of 1929-30.)
HŪVINA-HIPPARAGI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the proper left wall (outside) of the Hanumān temple.

This is dated the month of Śrāvaṇa śu. 4, Friday. These details are not enough
to fix the date.

It records a gift of land to the Jaina deity, Tīrthadēva of Iṅgaḷēśvara, for feeding
the Jaina monks and nuns on the occasion of the death anniversary of Remmarasa's
father by Habu Viṭharasa, Remmarasa and Rēcharasa. The gift was received by
Dēvachandra in the presence of the preceptor Dharmakīrti Bhaṭāraka.

inscription.head:
No. 643.
(B.K. No. 112 of 1929-30.)
NADIHĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in the Doḍḍamane field two miles from the village.
It states that the land belonged to the god Kapāḷēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 644.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1930-31.)
DOṆŪR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No 284.

It records a gift of land to the god Svayaṁbhu Mūlasthāna for offerings, by
the Two Five Hundred of Iṅgaḷēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 645.
(B.K. No. 27 of 1930-31.)
MĀRAKABBINAHAḶḶI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 119.
It records a gift of land to Aipa, son of the barber Muda, by the Five Hundred.

inscription.head:
No. 646.
(B.K. No. 31 of 1930-31.)
RABBINAHĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up near the village gate.
It seems to refer to a gift to the god Chandrēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 647.
(B.K. No. 32 of 1930-31.)
SATIHĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Iśvara temple.

It records a gift of land to the brahmapuri of Gōpāḷadēva of Chandaṇagēri at
Iṅgaḷēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 648.
(B.K. No. 34 of 1930-31.)
VANDĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 39.

It states that the land was the sarvanamasya gift to the god Jalaśayana of
Muttage.

inscription.head:
No. 649.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Mallēśvara temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Durmukhi, Āśvija śu. 10, Tuesday. On palaeogra-
phical grounds the record may be assigned to the 14th century in which case the
details correspond to 1356 A.D., October 4.

It states that Gabbināyaka, an officer of Side-nāyaka of Handiganūr had a ruined
temple at Soraṭūr renovated and installed the deity Basavēśvara therein.

l.ka: ḍidanu maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 650.
(B.K. No. 226 of 1928-29.)
HĀLKUSUGAL, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone lying in the Hanumantadēva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1292, Sunday, the other details being lost. The Śaka year
corresponds to 1370-71 A.D.

States that the memorial stone marks the spot where a warrior fought and died.
The name of the hero and other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 651.
(B.K. No. 440 of 1926.)
MANGUṆḌI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a sati-stone set up in a field called Siddhaliṅga-hakkala.

This is dated Kālayuktākshi, Śrāvaṇa-amāvāsye, Wednesday. As the inscription
can be assigned, on palaeographical grounds, to the 14th century the above details
may be equated to 1384 A.D., August 17, if the cyclic year is taken as Raktākshi.
If the cyclic year is interpreted as Kālayukti, the details do not correspond to neither
of the Kālayukti years occurring in the century.
It records the death of Nāḍaprabhu Mallappa of Haḷigeṟe.

l.note: This line is engraved on

inscription.head:
No. 652.
(B.K. No. 241 of 1927-28.)
HONNAKAṬṬI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the big stone-well in front of the Māruti temple.
It records a gift of money and oil by Saṁgaṇagauḍa of Honnāyakanakaṭe.

inscription.head:
No. 653.
(B.K. No. 250 of 1927-28.)
GADDANAKĒRI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the side of the Bagalkot road, a furlong east of the village.

It states that the land was a gift to Mōnapataiya, son of Māḷigappa, for having
constructed a tank.

inscription.head:
No. 654.
(B.K. No. 286 of 1927-28.)
BĒVŪR, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone built into the wall of the Parvatappa temple.
This is dated the cyclic year Pramādi. Other details are not given.

It records the death of Nāraṁṇa in a fight and refers to the setting up of the
memorial for the warrior.

inscription.head:
No. 655.
(B.K. No. 143 of 1928-29.)
CHOḶACHAGUḌḌA, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Kalamēśvara temple.

It seems to state that the entire yield of areca-nuts from the area was purchased
by the three Goravas of the goddess and Jaṅgama Yavōja.

inscription.head:
No. 656.
(B.K. No. 188 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar to the right of the southern entrance (outer side) into the
Amṛitēśvara temple.
It seems to condemn the evil of gambling.

inscription.head:
No. 657.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1929-30.)
BASARKŌḌ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the compound wall of Kari-Basappa temple.

It records a saying that there is none who kills a Leñka and none who eats
the flesh of a dog.

inscription.head:
No. 658.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1929-30.)
GUḌIHĀḶ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone standing at the boundary between this village and Kuṇṭōji.
It states that the land was the gift of Basavarājadēva of Saṁgamanātha.

l.note: This is followed by some

inscription.head:
No. 659.
(B.K. No. 16 of 1929-30.)
GUṆḌKARJGI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a small slab lying in the Hanumān temple.

It seems to refer to the setting up of the nisidhi memorial of a person (name
lost), disciple of Chandrakīrti of Dēsīya-gaṇa, probably by Jayakīrti.

inscription.head:
No. 660.
(B.K. No. 43 of 1929-30.)
MAILĒŚVAR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a Nāga-stone set up near a temple outside the village.
It seems to record the salutation to the Nāga by a Siddha.

inscription.head:
No. 661.
(B.K. No. 64 of 1929-30.)
SULTĀNPUR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a mutilated stone near the chāvaḍi.
This fragmentary record mentions Toṇḍarasa.

inscription.head:
No. 662.
(B.K. No. 84 of 1929-30.)
BĀGEVĀḌI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone in the stream near the field of Basappa Gurapa Murā.
It states that the land was the property of the goldsmith Bāgōja.

inscription.head:
No. 663.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1930-31.)
MUḶVĀḌ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of a cavern called the gavi.
This is dated the cyclic year Īśvara, [Bhādra]padā śu. 1. It cannot be verified.

It seems to record a gift to a deity. It refers to Muḷivāḍa, Alama Rahuta and
the Aṟuvatuvakalu.

inscription.head:
No. 664.
(B.K. No. 152 of 1928-29.)
KAṬGĒRI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the wall of the Hanumantadēva temple.
It mentions Bommūttiya, son of Giḍapa of Biḷekudari.

inscription.head:
No. 665.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1927-28.)
CHYĀGIRAKĒRI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On one of the two sluices of the tank.

This is dated the cyclic year Tāraṇa, Vaiśākha śu. 5, Monday. As the record
may be assigned palaeographically to the 15th century, the date may be equated
to 1404 A.D., April 14.

It states that the sluice of the tank was set up with due ceremony by Gōpaṇṇa.
son of Māsaverggaḍe Chikkaṇṇa-Upādhya. Sēnabōva Varadapaiya paid thirty
Pratāpa-Varāhas towards its expenses and it was constructed by the mason Tipōja.

inscription.head:
No. 666.
(B.K. No. 95 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into a wall inside the Chandranātha-basti.

This is dated Śaka 1342, Śārvari, Vaiśākha śu. 14, Vaḍḍavāra (i.e. Saturday),
corresponding to 1420 A.D., April 27.

It records the death of Buḷiseṭṭiya Kēshappa of Muḷugunda, who was a lay
disciple of the preceptor Hēmasēna of Sēna-gaṇa. The teacher bore the praśas11ti
Rāya-rājaguru, Maṇḍalāchārya, Vādi-vādīsvara, etc.

l.ka: nistikeya kalla nilisidaru maṁgaḷa mahā śrī

inscription.head:
No. 667.
(B.K. No. 94 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into a wall inside the Chandranātha basti.

This is dated Śaka 1343, Plava, Bhādrapada śu. 9, Friday, corresponding to
1421 A.D., September 5, Friday, f.d.t. °14.

It records the death of Nāgarasa, son of Sēnabōva, Heggaḍe Maduvarasa who
was a minister and Bāhattara-niyōgādhipati of Tiḷakarasa of Soraṭūr. The earlie
portion, in Sanskrit, records the death by the vow of sannyāsa of Amṛita, disciple
of Bhaṭṭārakadēva of Balātkāra-gaṇa. The details of the date of the latter's death
are the same as those given above but without the Śaka year.

inscription.head:
No. 668.
(B.K. No. 71 of 1929-30.)
TANGAḌIGI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone standing near the Hanumān temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Krōdhana, Śrāvaṇa-amāvāsye, Monday. Palaeo-
graphically the record might be assigned to the 15th century. The date would thus
correspond to 1445 A.D., August 2, Monday, f.d.t. °53.

It records a gift of land for offerings to the god Kapilasidda of Dēvināyakara-
maṭha by Mallināyaka of Barige for feeding the Jaṁgamas in the name of Kapaḍiya
Saṁgama.

l.note: Lines 11 to 14 are engraved on
Lines 15 and onward are engraved on a

inscription.head:
No. 669.
(B.K. No. 88 of 1929-30.)
HATTIRIKIHĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone in the front wall of the Kallinātha temple.

This is dated Śaka 1410, Kīlaka, Kārttika śu. 10, corresponding to 1488 A.D.
October 15 (not verifiable).

It seems to record a gift to the god Kallinātha of Hatrikihāḷa by Nandigauṇḍa
and others.

inscription.head:
No. 670.
(B.K. No. 27 of 1926-27.)
KAḶASĀPURA, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the flooring of the Vīrabhadra temple.
The extant portion refers to Chaitra śu 10, all other details of date being lost.

It seems to record a gift of money to the god Sōmanātha. The inscription is
damaged.

l.note: The record

inscription.head:
No. 671.
(B.K. No. 99 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the house of the archaka of the Siddhēśvara temple.

It records a gift of land to the priest Siddarāmaiyya Voḍeya for the worship of
the god Siddayyadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 672.
(B.K. No. 106 of 1926-27.)
KALLŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Kallappa temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Plavaṅga, Mārgaśira śu. 15. The details cannot be
verified. It records the gift of the village Kallūr or Kallāpura in Muḷugunda-vaḷita to
Bommaṇaiya for the benefit of the god Huttada Mallikārjuna of Gēkinakaṭṭe by
Chavuḍi Kadamba-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 673.
(B.K. No. 80 of 1927-28.)
DŌṆI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in a field about a furlong from the Dōṇikavva temple.

It records a gift of land to the god Śrīpatidēva by a son of Śrīdharāchārya, whose
name is not mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 674.
(B.K. No. 251 of 1927-28.)
GADDANAKĒRI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the side of the Bagalkot road about two furlongs from the village.

It seems to state that the land was a gift to the tank constructed by Rāghavaji
Bhīmapa Dēsāyi.

inscription.head:
No. 675.
(B.K. No. 262 of 1927-28.)
HONNIHĀḶ, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Hanumān temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Śrīmukha, Māgha śu. 1. The details of date cannot
be verified. It records the gift of a village to the Dombas. The epigraph is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 676.
(B.K. No. 271 of 1927-28.)
SITIMANI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone bearing two foot-prints in front of the temple on the hill.
It records a gift to the god Rāmanātha. The donor's name is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 677.
(B.K. No. 139 of 1928-29.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in a field about a mile on the road to Ramdrug.
It records a gift of land to Nāvila Dēchaya of Bādāvi-sthaḷa by a Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 678.
(B.K. No. 190 of 1928-29.)
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Amṛitēśvara temple.

It states that the image of Nandi was the work of Kannōja, son of Yeḷemēla
Basaōja.

l.ka: yeḷemēla basa·ōjana maga kaṁnōja māḍida naṁdi śrī

inscription.head:
No. 679.
(B.K. No. 243 of 1928-29.)
SISUVINAHAḶḶI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in Survey No. 66.

It states that the village of Kṛishṇarāyasamudra was granted as an agrahāra to
the Brāhmaṇas and it was indicated by the stone with the figure of Vāmana bearing
the epigraph. The record was engraved by Kariyōja son of Dēvōja.

inscription.head:
No. 680.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1929-30.)
BĀGĒVĀḌI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone in the field of Kañchagāra Gaṅgappa.
It reads ḍanillakaṭa. The purport cannot be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 681.
(B.K. No. 256 of 1927-28.)
SORKOP, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two faces of a stone lying in front of the village gate.
It mentions a Barigi Malli-nāyaka and refers to Dēsāyi.

l.note: The remaining portion is badly

inscription.head:
No. 682.
(B.K. No. 154 of 1928-29.)
KERŪR, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the bank of the well called Huṇasimarada-bhāvi.

This is dated [Śaka] 1431, Śukla, Kārttika ba., the other details being lost.
The Śaka year corresponds to 1509-10 A.D.
It seems to record a gift of land to the deity Basava by a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 683.
(B.K. No. 20 of 1927-28.)
KARAKIKAṬṬI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of the Kariyavva temple.

This is dated Śaka 14., Yuva, Śrāvaṇa ba. 1. With the help of the cyclic year
the Śaka year can be restored as 1437. Thus the details would correspond to 1515 A.D.
July 26. The details cannot, however, be verified.

It records a gift of land to the goddess Uḍachādēvī by Mahāmaṇḍaleśvara Taṟimaleya
Kōnērirāju • • • • • Chinnayadēva Mahā-arasu.

l.ka: laṁ bhavētu yi kallanu niṁtu hākisidavaru
siriya benaka sivapana makaḷa makaḷu tapade naḍedu

inscription.head:
No. 684.
(B.K. No. 20 of 1930-31.)
MARAGŌḶI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up near the Hanumān temple.

The last line of the inscription seems to refer to the date Śaka 1444, which corres-
ponds to 1522-23 A.D.
It mentions Dēvanāyaka, son of Bīchināyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 685.
(B.K. No. 120 of 1926-27.)
NĪLGUNDA, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in a field about a furlong to the west of the village.

This is dated Śaka 1445, Svabhānu, Māgha, ba. 30, Friday, corresponding to 1524
A.D., February 4. The weekday, however, was Thursday.

It records a gift of land to the god Siddhēśvara of Muḷugunda by Vemgaḷapa,
son of Bāgila Malapa-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 686.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1927-28.)
MUSHṬIKOP, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar lying in the Venkaṭēśadēva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Śrāvaṇa śu. 5, corresponding to 1532 A.D.,
July 7. The details cannot be verified.
It mentions Bārikara Malla.

l.note: The record

inscription.head:
No. 687.
(B.K. No. 138 of 1926-27.)
HUYILGŌḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in the Māruti temple.

This is dated Śaka 1458, Durmukhi, Bhādrapada ba. 10, Sunday, corresponding
to 1536 A.D., September 10.

It records the death of Badakala Nēmo-nāyaka and his two sons, the kāṇāchi-
kāṟas of Taḷavāṟike, in a dispute over the boundary of the villages Hūligōḷa and
Gāvaravāḍa. The stone was set up in memory of the deceased by Giriyapa-nāyaka
another son of Nēmo-nāyaka.

l.ka: nanāyaka vobanāyaka yī mūvaru taṁma taḷavāṟike kā-
tiṣṭaya māḍidaṁ nēmonāyakana magana giriyapa nāya
l.note: The stone is broken off after this.

inscription.head:
No. 688.
(B.K. No. 217 of 1926-27.)
ABBIGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone leaning against the left wall of the temple of Uḍachavva.

This is dated the cyclic year Plava, Bhādrapada śu. 10, Tuesday. As the
epigraph can be assigned to the 16th century, on palaeographical grounds, these details
may be equated to 1541 A.D., August 30.

It records the death of Timmaṇa in a fight and gift of Bīramānya in the memory
of the deceased.

l.note: The continuation is

inscription.head:
No. 689.
(B.K. No. 3 of 1929-30.)
BAḶAGĀNŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone near the Hanumān temple.

The inscription first mentions Hēmaḷambi and then Śaka 1466. The said cyclic
year occurring in Śaka 1459 corresponded to 1537 A.D. The given Śaka year
corresponds to 1544 A.D. The second date seems to be the date of confirmation
of the grant made previously.

It records a gift of land as sarvamānya to Kaṁpeya-voḍeya, the Jaṅgama of
Nālvatubāḍa by the Aruvattokkalu of Baḷahanūr and Dōgi-nāyaka of Haṅga-
rige.

inscription.head:
No. 690.
(B.K. No. 173 of 1926-27.)
RŌṆ, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in front of the Drōṇāchārya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṁga, Kārttika śu. 7, Friday, corresponding to
1547 A.D., October 21.
The record stops with the mention of the date.

inscription.head:
No. 691.
(B.K. No. 37 of 1930-31.)
YĀḶVĀR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the compound wall of the Sōmēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1479 Piṅgaḷa, Vaiśākha ba. 5, Sunday, Mūlā-nakshatra,
corresponding to 1557 A.D., April 18.

It records the construction of a step-well called Sōma-tīrtha in the south-eastern
corner of the Sōmēśvara temple by Chenna-Basavantarāya. This Chenna-Basavanta-
rāya is described as the grandson of Nāganāyaka of Pākanāṭi-kula, chief of Yāḷavara
in the region of Vijayapura. The construction was undertaken with the approval
of Rāmaṇa-nāyaka who was the junior uncle of the donor and administrative head of
the place.

l.ka: vayiśākha vadi paṁcami bhānuvāra mūla nakṣa-
kirināykara putra nāgaṇa gauḍa svahasta svagrāmada kara-

inscription.head:
No. 692.
(B.K. No. 134 of 1926-27.)
HUYILGŌḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the platform of the Kalamēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1484, Dundubhi, Pushya śu. 15, Saturday, lunar eclipse
corresponding to 1563 A.D., January 9.

It records the restoration of the gift of land in favour of the god Mūlasthāna
Kalinātha or Kallayyadēva of Hulagoḷa by Mutyapa, the Pārupatyagāra of the place,
for the merit of Vīrappa-nāyaka. The various services to be maintained out of the
income from the gift are enumerated in detail.

inscription.head:
No. 693.
(B.K. No. 468 of 1926.)
AḶṆĀVAR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in a field of the Liṅgāyata-maṭha.

This is dated Śaka 1478, Krōdhana, Āśvīja ba. 14. The Śaka year cited appears
to be a mistake for 1487, as Krōdhana coincided with the latter date. With this
correction the details may be equated to 1565 A.D., October 14.

It records the death by the rite of sallēkhana of Chikkamalliseṭṭi of Hubbaḷḷi
after fasting for 21 days.

l.ka: maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 694.
(B.K. No. 19 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone in front of the Uḍachavva temple.

It seems to record some legendary account of the deity Uḍiche who is said to
have taken human form in a female devotee bearing the name.

l.ka: śrīguruvē • | ·uḍacaṁma huṭida modalāgi mataṁgiya pūjeyanu maḍi • •

yikāyiṁ • lāgi ·ātana maga ma[rā]mināyakana maga [bai]renāyakana maga
·uḍica • •

inscription.head:
No. 695.
(B.K. No. 92 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Pārśvanātha temple.

It states that the Jaina preceptor Sahasrakīrti, disciple of Lalitakīrti, of Desi-
gaṇa, Pustaka-gachchha and Hanasōge-anvaya, stayed unshaken inside the Pārśvanātha
Jinālaya which was set on fire by the Mohammadans and was burnt to death. He
thus vindicated the greatness of the Jaina faith.

l.note: These two lines are engraved in ṇāgari characters.
The inscription is incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 696.
(B.K. No. 133 of 1926-27.)
KŌṬAVUMACHIGI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Jaina basti.
It states that the village Umachigi was also called Sadāśivarāya-samudra.

inscription.head:
No. 697.
(B.K. No. 178 of 1926-27.)
RŌṆ, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of the Hanumān temple near the Dharmaśālā.
This is dated the cyclic year Krōdhi, Mārgaśira, śu. 15. The details cannot be verified.

It records the confirmation of an earlier gift of land to Nārasimhadāsa, son of
Goḍasala Koṇḍadāsa for the worship of the god Hanumanta. The gift was confirmed
by Malarasaya who was the agent of Immaḍi Manvanāyaka of the Gujala family of
Kushṭige and Pārupatyagāra of Bādāvi-sīme.

l.ka: śrī krōdhi saṁ | ma¨rgaśira śu 15 lu kuṣṭi-

inscription.head:
No. 698.
(B.K. No. 213 of 1926-27.)
MUDENGUḌI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone near a public well outside the village.

It refers to a construction, probably of the well, by Jaṅgamaya Sōmagauṇḍa,
son of Murārinātha-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 699.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1927-28.)
YĀONGAL, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the wall of the backyard of Hire-maṭha.

It records a gift of land in Hūvinahaḍagile to Narahari-bhaṭṭa, son of Chimmarasa,
of Viśvāmitra-gōtra and Ṛik-śākhā.

inscription.head:
No. 700.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1927-28.)
GAJĒNDRAGAḌ, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the stone gate leading to the fort.

It refers to the regime of Hajarata Nabāba and cites the date San 76, Hijara,
Plava. The record falls approximately in the 16th century.

It states that the gate and the bastion of the fort were caused to be made by
Murārāje Nilage, the officer-in-charge.

inscription.head:
No. 701.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1927-28.)
MUNDARGI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a stone bearing two footprints in the court-yard of the
Śiva temple on the hillock.

It seems to register a gift by certain officers and representatives of different coM-
munities. The epigraph is incomplete.

l.note: The record stops here.

inscription.head:
No. 702.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1927-28.)
BYĀLAVĀḌIGI, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in Survey No. 27.

It seems to record a gift of money to Chika-Vīraṇa. The epigraph is damaged
and the details including the date are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 703.
(B.K. No. 82 of 1927-28.)
ĀLŪR, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Nāgēśvara temple.

It records a fixed monthly gift of money for offerings to the deity Durgā-Para-
mēśvari by the Mahājanas.

inscription.head:
No. 704.
(B.K. No. 249 of 1927-28.)
VĪRĀPUR, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the stone door-frame of the village gate.

The inscription which is damaged mentions Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Narasarājayya.
It seems to record a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 705.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1928-29.)
CHIKKA-ĀDĀPUR, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of the Hanumān temple.

It seems to record a gift to a god by Ēchami-nāyaka and another gift to Kula
karaṇi Tiṁmarasa. The record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 706.
(B.K. No. 132 of 1928-29.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a boulder in the by-path at the right of the passage leading up to the northern
fort.

This is dated the cyclic year Krō[dhana], Māgha śu. 10. The date cannot be
verified.

It seems to record the construction of a fort by Baiyyapēndra. It mentions
Kṛishṇa-bhūpati. The epigraph is badly damaged.

l.note: The rest of the inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 707.
(B.K. No. 134 of 1928-29.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in a shrine of Śiva by the side of the Bhūtanātha temple.
It states that (the pillar) was a gift of Tirumale, son of Janappa of Hoṁganūr.

inscription.head:
No. 708.
(B.K. No. 142 of 1928-29.)
CHIRĀḶKOP, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying on the road to Gōvankop at a distance of half a mile.
It states that it is the north-eastern boundary stone bearing the figure of a liṅga.

inscription.head:
No. 709.
(B.K. No. 144 of 1928-29.)
DHĀṆAKA-SIRUR, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying on the way to Maṇṇēri about a furlong from the village.

This is dated the cyclic year Paridhāvi, Māgha śu. 11. The details cannot be
verified.

It seems to record a gift of land by Timmarasa, the Nāḍa-gauḍa of Dāṇaka-
siruūra when Kōna-Rāchayya was administering the tract.

l.ka: ga śu 11 lū kōna rācayana rājya-

inscription.head:
No. 710.
(B.K. No. 216 of 1928-29.)
BELVAṬIGI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 117.

It records the construction of a tank by the sons of Sēnabōva Lakaṇa, who
received a gift of land and house-site for the work done. The epigraph is damaged
in the beginning.

l.ka: yī kereya kaṭisidake yeraḍu maru vola mane 2

l.note: The

inscription.head:
No. 711.
(B.K. No. 225 of 1928-29.)
HĀLKUSUGAL, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the Hirēmaṭha.

It seems to state that the land bearing the stone belonged to the Hirē-maṭha of
Nauvālagunda (i.e. modern Navalagund).

inscription.head:
No. 712.
(B.K. No. 248 of 1928-29.)
TIRLĀPUR, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in a field about a mile to the south of the village.

It states that Viṭhalāpura was another name of Sīrekōṇa. The inscription is in
Nāgarī characters.

inscription.head:
No. 713.
(B.K. No. 1 of 1929-30.)
ALKOPPAR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone standing before the Hanumān temple.
Only the name of the month Mārgaśira is preserved in the date portion.

It seems to record a gift of land to the god Hanumān of Ālakopara. The epigraph
is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 714.
(B.K. No. 88 of 1928-29.)
KOṆṆŪR, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into a wall of Bīradēva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1547, Krōdhana, daśami, Saturday. The details cannot be
verified. The Śaka year corresponds to 1625-26 A.D.

It records the construction of the Chandraśālā by Nāgaṇṇa-gauḍa, son of Bhūsaṇ-
ṇa-gauḍa of Koṇaganūr.

inscription.head:
No. 715.
(B.K. No. 30 of 1929-30.)
IṄGAḶAGĒRI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken stone lying before the Hanumān temple.

This is dated Śaka 1589, Prabha, Śrāvaṇa ba. 2. The name of the cyclic year
appears to have been engraved wrongly for Parābhava. If so, it corresponds to Śaka
1589, current. The details may thus be equated to 1666 A.D., August 6, though they
cannot be verified.

Except for the invocation to Gaṇādhipati in the beginning, followed by the date,
the rest of the inscription is obliterated.

inscription.head:
No. 716.
(B.K. No. 93 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Chandranātha basti.

This is dated Śaka 1597, Rākshasa, Bhādrapada ba. 5, Sunday, corresponding
to 1675 A.D., August 29, Sunday, f.d.t. °47.

It states that Banadāṁbike, wife of Kulakaraṇi Nāgabhūpa had the image of
Ādinātha consecrated, as the temple was polluted by the Mohammadans entering into
of forcibly.

l.ka: dhakārō ravi || saddha¨rmmāṁbeṇevakki rājipa ravi | sa¨rvvasya nētrā ravi | śa-

l.note: This line seems to have been added on a later date.

inscription.head:
No. 717.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a foot step in the temple of Siddhēśvara.
This is dated Śaka 1617 corresponding to 1695-96 A.D.

The record which is in Nāgarī characters and Marāṭhi language mentions the
construction of the eastern doorway by Khaṇḍērāva Timmāji.

l.de: khaṁḍairāva timājī purvaṁ dvāra kele saka 2627

inscription.head:
No. 718.
(B.K. No. 224 of 1926-27.)
ABBĪGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the compound of Jyōtirliṅga temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Svabhānu, Bhādrapada ba. 30. The details cannot
be verified.

It seems to record a gift of land by [Mādi]dēva-nāyaka of Barige for maintaining
a charitable picotta. The inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 719.
(B.K. No. 225 of 1926-27.)
ABBĪGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock in the Yemmikallu field in the village.

This is dated the cyclic year Tāraṇa, Śrāvaṇa śu. 11. The details cannot be
verified.

It records a gift of land to a maṭha for the merit of his teacher Parvatadēva by
Mahārāja Immaḍi Barigeya Dēva-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 720.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1927-28.)
UṆACHGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a stone set up in Ayyanakoḷḷa near the Īśvara temple
on the Gajēndragaḍ hill.

The date of the record is lost. It records a gift to Chenabasapa by Dēsāyi
Surapa-Liṅgapa.

inscription.head:
No. 721.
(B.K. No. 38 of 1927-28.)
HARLĀPUR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the Hanumantadēva temple.

This undated record registers a gift of land and refers to Kāche-nāyaka of
Nāraṇapura and to a dispute in Hariharapura-sīme.

inscription.head:
No. 722.
(B.K. No. 216 of 1928-29.)
BEḶVAṬIGI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in Survey No. 117.

It speaks of the construction of a tank by several Gauḍas and others. It also
refers to a gift of land and house for the work. Dēsāyi Nēmaṇa and Dēsa-kulakarani
Gōpaṇa are mentioned.

l.ka: yi kereya kaṭisidake yeraḍu maru hola mana 2

inscription.head:
No. 723.
(B.K. No. 24 of 1929-30.)
HIRĒMURĀḶ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the basement of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Durmukhi, Āshāḍha śu. 10. The details cannot be
verified.
It seems to register a gift to the god Saṅgamanātha. The inscription is damaged.

l.note: The rest of the inscription

inscription.head:
No. 724.
(B.K. No. 72 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, BIḶGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into a wall of the well called Ārettina-bhāvi.

This is dated Śaka 1630, Sarvadhāri, Mārggaśira śu. 10, Thursday, Uttarā-
bhādrapadā, corresponding to 1708 A.D., November 11, Thursday, f.d.n. °43.

It states that Visāji Mahādēva Paṇḍita of Gārgya-gōtra had a well constructed
in the northern region of Biḷgi. The record is in Marāṭhi language and Nāgarī
characters.

l.de: śrī lakṣmīṁnṛsiṁhāya namaḥ || svasti śrī nṛpa śālivāhana śāke
[1]630 sarvadhārī nāma saṁvatsara mārgaśorṣa śutdha daśamī ḍuruvā-
rāṣṭra svakaṣda rjiṁta vitteṁ viligīsa ·uttarabhagī vihi[ś]dīrghiṁkā
vāṁdhi vilī yā kāmāsa ·adhyakṣa ·apalā pinṛvya pauna kṛṣṇājī
raghunāyeṁ ·āraṁbhāpāsūna yākt pūrṇahoya tāvat parāmarśa ke-
lā ·apaṇā savaṁśa paraṁparā rājagṛhā pāsūna bhūmi pākśera sa-
rvasveṁ dilhī ·aseṁ yā madhyeṁ vāpī malā kelā ·ahe yā bhūmisa ·apu-
lyā vaṁśajā vinā ·anayajo dravya dhānya phale[ca]athavā ·āropita
phalita puṣpita vṛkṣa dhedakarīla to nārakī hoyīla | ḥ svadattā dvigu
ṇaṁ puṣyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ || paradattāpahrāreṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavet ||

inscription.head:
No. 725.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another stone built into a wall of the well called Ārettina-bhāvi.

The contents of this epigraph are identical with those of the above inscription
except that this is written in Kannaḍa language and characters. The author of the well
is mentioned here as Visājipanta, son of Pātāḷaśāyi Mahādēva.

l.ka: svasti śrī nrupa śālivāhanaśakē 1630 sa¨rvvadhāri saṁ-
yā māḍi vaṁdu [pāvina kriśi]koṭu yiddāre yidake dā-

inscription.head:
No. 726.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a third stone built into a wall of the well called Ārettina-bhāvi.

This record in Nāgarī characters is composed in Sanskrit verse. Its contents are
the same as of the above two epigraphs.

l.de: ke vatsare sarvadhāriṇi || mārgaśīrṣe site pakṣe da-
vau me yo vinārvaśrayaṁ dātā bhokā [sa] nāralī || cha ||

inscription.head:
No. 727.
(B.K. No. 26 of 1927-28.)
UṆACHGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the arch-stone of the entrance to Kṛishṇamūrti temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Plavaṅga, Mārgaśira ba. 14, Monday. On palaeogra-
phical grounds the record may be assigned approximately to the 18th century in which
case the details may be equated to 1788 A.D., January 7.

It records the construction of the Vēṇugōpāla temple by Aiyapa, the Pēṭī Kotawāla
of the Gājēndragaḍ fort. The inscription is in Nāgari characters and Kannaḍa
language.

l.de: kī le gaje dragaḍādā peṭī kotavālā

inscription.head:
No. 728.
(B.K. No. 183 of 1928-29.)
YEṆḌIGĒRI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Basavaṇṇa temple about a mile from the village.

It seems to describe the glory of Basavaṇṇa (i.e., Bull-god) and some miracle
attributed to Him.

l.ka: da || hādi basanāgi nā·u hōgadilla muṁda

inscription.head:
No. 729.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1929-30.)
HIRĒMURĀḶ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the basement of Saṅgamēśvara temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Durmukhi, Vaīśākha ba. 2. The details cannot be
verified.
It records a gift of land and house-site to the god Saṅgama by Bhōja-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 730.
(B.K. No. 26 of 1929-30.)
HIRŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the proper left door-jamb of the sanctuary in the Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

It records a gift of land made in the name of the god Śaṅkara of Hiru-ūru (i.e.,
Hirūr).

inscription.head:
No. 731.
(B.K. No. 28 of 1929-30.)
HULLŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Rāmaliṅga temple.

It seems to state that the pillar marks the boundary line between the temple of
Mūlasthāna and the smithy of Gōpaṇa, son of Bayichōja of Naḍugaṇḍe.

inscription.head:
No. 732.
(B.K. No. 93 of 1928-29.)
NĀGRĀḶ, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken stone lying in the house near the Hanumān temple.

This is dated San. 1241. The reference might be to the Sūr-san, in which case it
may be equated to 1841 A.D.

It seems to refer to the planting of trees. The inscription is written in Nāgarī
characters and Marāṭhī language.

inscription.head:
No. 733.
(B.K. No. 247 of 1928-29.)
TALI-MORAB, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the platform of the Pañchamukhi Hanumantadēva temple.

These seem to be pilgrims' records. One in Kannḍa characters mentions the
name Dattātrēya Avadhūta. This is followed by Nāgarī writing recording the date
Śaka 1763, Plava, Vaiśākha va. 30. This corresponds to 1841 A.D., May 20.

In the upper portion of the slab is engraved a short inscription in Arabic comprising
religious text with an amulet of the figure 20 at the end (which is also incorrect), used
for the supplication of the dead. This leads one to infer that the record is probably
an epitaph of religious interest only.

l.de: || śrī || śape da 763 varṣe pravartamāne plava saṁvatsara vaiśākha vadya || 30 ||

inscription.head:
No. 734.
(B.K. No. 235 of 1928-29.)
MORAB, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of the Basavaṇṇa temple.
This record, in late characters, seems to refer to a gift of land. It is damaged.

edition.h4:
THE FOLLOWING IS A LIST OF INSCRIPTIONS WHOSE TEXTS
COULD NOT BE GIVEN ON ACCOUNT OF THEIR UNSATISFACTORY
CONDITION.

inscription.head:
No. 735.
(B.K. No. 467 of 1926.)
NIGADI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone at Bammarāsi-koppa, a deserted village near Nigadi.
Worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 736.
(B.K. No. 166 of 1926-27.)
KARḌI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the chāvaḍi.

Badly damaged; circa 16th century. Mentions Malaya-voḍeya and seems to
record a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 737.
(B.K. No. 206 of 1926-27.)
NIḌGUNDI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of the Basavaṇṇa temple.
Worn out; in Chālukyan characters of about the 12th century.

inscription.head:
No. 738.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1927-28.)
CHIKKAMAṆṆŪR, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone leaning against the front wall of the Hanumān temple.

Worn out; circa 13th century. The name of the month Māgha in a Śaka year
can be read.

inscription.head:
No. 739.
(B.K. No. 6 of 1927-28.)
SANDIGWĀḌ, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero stone set up in front of the Kalamēśvara temple.
Damaged; circa 14th century. Seems to record the death of a chief.

inscription.head:
No. 740.
(B.K. No. 10 of 1927-28.)
KAVAJGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken stone lying in the village chāvaḍi.
Damaged; circa 12th century. Mentions a warrior.

inscription.head:
No. 741.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1927-28.)
KAVAJGĒRI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in front of the Siddhaliṅgēśvara temple.

Damaged; circa 12th century. Seems to record a gift in commemoration of
a hero who died in a battle.

inscription.head:
No. 742.
(B.K. No. 263 of 1927-28.)
MANAHAḶḶI, BAGALKOT TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in front of the Māruti temple.
Worn out; circa 12th century. Only the imprecation can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 743.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1928-29.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up near the well at Huchchimalliguḍi.
Worn out; circa 9th century.

inscription.head:
No. 744.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1928-29.)
GAÑJIHĀḶ, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in the gate of the Brahman-gallī.
Badly damaged; in modern characters.

inscription.head:
No. 745.
(B.K. No. 43 of 1928-29.)
HĒRŪR, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fragment of a stone in the compound of the Hanumān temple.
Mutilated and damaged; circa 12th century. Seems to record a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 746.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1928-29.)
KAMATGI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in front of the village chāvaḍi.
Fragmentary and damaged; in modern characters.

inscription.head:
No. 747.
(B.K. No. 61 of 1928-29.)
MARŌḶ, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab inside the Rāmaliṅga temple.
Worn out; circa 11th century.

inscription.head:
No. 748.
(B.K. No. 76 of 1928-29.)
BĪḶGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a mutilated stone lying in the compound of the Native General Library.

It contains a few mystic syllables like Hrīṁ, Klīṁ, Hrōṁ in Nāgarī and also
a damaged Yantra with similar syllables in Kannaḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 749.
(B.K. No. 94 of 1928-29.)
TEGGI, BILGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in Survey No. 1.
In late Mōḍī characters.

inscription.head:
No. 750.
(B.K. No. 160 of 1928-29.)
MUSHṬIGĒRI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the platform of the maṭha.
Worn out; circa 14th century.

inscription.head:
No. 751.
(B.K. No. 161 of 1928-29.)
NĀGANŪR, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the Hanumantadēva temple.

It contains an astronomical Chakra in Kannaḍa characters of about the 11th
century.

inscription.head:
No. 752.
(B.K. No. 185 of 1928-29.)
YEṆḌIGĒRI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in the house of Aḍaveppa Piṅjār.
Peeled off; circa 14th century. Seems to register a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 753.
(B.K. No. 242 of 1928-29.)
SIRŪR, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying near the old village gate.
Fragmentary and damaged; in late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 754.
(B.K. No. 9 of 1929-30.)
BIJJŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone set up before the Hanumān temple.
Damaged; circa 14th century.

inscription.head:
No. 755.
(B.K. No. 33 of 1929-30.)
JEṬṬIGI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder in a field near the village.
Damaged; in late Mōḍī characters.

inscription.head:
No. 756-759.
(B.K. Nos. 48 to 51 of 1929-30.)
NĀLATVĀḌ, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in Jumma-Masjid.
For contents see A.R.S.I.E., 1929-30, Appendix E, p. 68.

inscription.head:
No. 760-763.
(B.K. Nos. 57 to 60 of 1929-30.)
RAKKASGI, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in local Masjid.
For contents see A.R.S.I.E., 1929-30, Appendix E, p. 68.

inscription.head:
No. 764.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1929-30.)
PHAḌĒKNŪR, MUDDEBIHAL TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a nandi-pillar standing in the Hanumān temple.
Worn out; circa 12th century.

inscription.head:
No. 765.
(B.K. No. 76 of 1929-30.)
ĀRESHANKAR, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone before the Śiva temple near the Hullūr channel.
Worn out; circa 15th century. Mentions Śivaliṅga.

inscription.head:
No. 766.
(B.K. No. 86 of 1929-30.)
BYĀLHĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a mutilated stone built into the gateway.
Worn out; circa 12th century. Mentions Bhādrapada śu. 5, Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 767.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1929-30.)
BYĀLHĀḶ, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder in the Ayyagaḷa field.
Worn out; in late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 768.
(B.K. No. 98 of 1929-30.)
MUTTIGI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab lying in front of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
Badly damaged; circa 12th century.

inscription.head:
No. 769.
(B.K. No. 120 of 1929-30.)
KUMṬĀ, HONAVAR TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab standing in a field.

The epigraph is badly damaged and worn out. The date is recorded in lines 19-20,
of which only Vikāri, Wednesday and Anurādha-nakshatra can be made out. It refers
to a lord of Banavāsī in line 7 and seems to record a gift of land. Mentions a Nāraṇa-
bhaṭṭa in lines 39-40 and taxes like birāḍa and siddāya.

inscription.head:
No. 770.
(B.K. No. 2 of 1930-31.)
BHAIRAVĀḌIGI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fragment lying near the small Lakshmī temple.
Damaged; in late characters. Mentions a chief.

inscription.head:
No. 771.
(B.K. No. 21 of 1930-31.)
MANAGŌḶI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone set up in the compound of the Hanumān temple.
Damaged and worn out; circa 12th century.
